{"id":"1f786741-48ca-4044-87c8-cd2b15e41bab","identifier":"FRG-234","title":"Set up QA regression suite for AI Aquarium","description":"## Context

AI Aquarium M1 is complete. Before M2 starts, we need automated regression tests that verify all M1 features still work after any code change.

## What to do

1. Read the project description in Linear — it contains the cumulative feature spec (the 'Features' section). This is your test contract.
2. Set up a QA repo with the right testing framework. The debug dashboard at /debug.html is a DOM-only page (no canvas) — Playwright is recommended.
3. Write regression tests covering every feature in the spec. Each test should map to a specific feature.
4. Tests should run against the live URL: [https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html](<https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html>)
5. Consider CI integration (GitHub Actions) so tests run on every PR.

## Key Constraints

* The sim is AI-driven — character decisions are non-deterministic. Tests must account for this (wait for events, observe over time, not assert exact sequences).
* Some features require observation over multiple AI cycles (\\~30s each). Use speed controls (4x) to accelerate.
* The Chrome memory leak (FRG-226) means long-running browser sessions may OOM. Keep individual test runs concise.

## QA Repo

chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa (exists but mostly empty)

## Acceptance Criteria

* Test suite covers all features listed in the project description
* Tests pass against current live deployment
* Clear mapping between tests and features
* README explains how to run tests locally and in CI","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-234/set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-ai-aquarium","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-234-set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-ai-aquarium","createdAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:08.899Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:20.103Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":["testing"],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

USERMESSAGE #11

SUCCESS 
{"id":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","icon":null,"color":"#bec2c8","name":"AI Aquarium","summary":"AI characters living in a cozy pixel world","description":"## Goal

A living pixel world where AI characters interact with each other and objects autonomously. Main page shows the sim running — characters walking, talking, using objects. Debug page (/debug.html) shows the state machine internals: object states, character decisions, event log.

## Live URLs

* Game: [https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app](<https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app>)
* Debug: [https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html](<https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html>)

## Notes

Two repos exist:

* ai-aquarium-phaser (Phaser 3, active)
* ai-aquarium (vanilla canvas, archived reference)

---

## Features (cumulative — updated after each milestone)

### Characters

* 3 characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx (display names; sprite names Amelia, Lucy, Ash never shown to user)
* 48×96 sprites with walk/idle animations
* Characters navigate via A\\* pathfinding on walkable tile grid (19×13)
* No wall-clipping or teleportation

### AI Decision Engine

* LLM-driven decisions every \\~30 seconds per character
* 4 action types: interact (use object), talk (converse with character), move (walk to tile), idle (wander)
* AI receives: character state, nearby objects with availability, nearby characters with canTalk status
* Decisions include dialogue text and description

### Smart Objects (Slot System)

* 10 interactive objects: tea_set, kotatsu, stove, bonsai, fridge, bed, bookshelf, desk, easel, tv
* Each object has capacity (most=1, kotatsu=2, tv=2)
* Slot lifecycle: reserve (walking toward) → arrive (using) → release (done)
* Objects show capacity in debug UI: N/N free, pip indicators
* Capacity-1 blocking: second character cannot use a full object
* Multi-capacity: two characters can use kotatsu/tv simultaneously

### Object State Machines

* Each object has defined states with auto-transitions (e.g., tea_set: idle→brewing→ready→idle)
* State transitions fire on interaction and auto-advance on timers
* All 10 objects have verified state cycles

### Talk Handshaking

* Character requests talk → target accepts (if idle/walking) or rejects (if interacting/talking)
* On accept: both enter talking state, face each other, talk timer counts down
* On reject: REJECT event logged with reason
* canTalk filter: LLM only targets available characters
* Talk targets distributed across all characters

### Fallback Logic

* When AI changes a character's plan mid-walk, a FALLBACK plan changed event fires
* When an object is unavailable at decision time, FALLBACK unavailable event fires
* Characters pick alternative objects or go idle on fallback
* Object reservations released on plan change

### Debug Dashboard (/debug.html)

* Character cards: name, position, action, target, facing, hold timer, AI countdown
* Object cards: name, state, slot capacity with pip indicators
* Event log: AI decisions, state transitions, TALK, REJECT, FALLBACK events with timestamps
* Speed controls: 1x, 2x, 4x
* Reset button: clears all state, repositions characters, resets time

### Hold Timer

* Characters stay at objects for the full interaction duration
* Timer visible in debug UI, counts down correctly","url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/project/ai-aquarium-87b010097054","createdAt":"2026-02-22T21:28:08.681Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T23:20:49.995Z","startDate":"2026-02-28","targetDate":null,"labels":[],"initiatives":[],"lead":{},"status":{"id":"45fae361-8f51-4684-a479-68682e997bd2","name":"In Progress"},"teams":[{"id":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd","name":"Forge","key":"FRG"}],"resources":[{"type":"link","id":"c583652f-e50a-4619-bddb-6a2bd0967dc9","label":"Debug Page","url":"https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:06.445Z"},{"type":"link","id":"ac866cde-0ed4-4682-812f-122171ba4bdc","label":"QA Repo","url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:06.254Z"},{"type":"link","id":"b2c4d798-1455-4f20-a2e2-52113f6becf6","label":"GitHub","url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:06.052Z"},{"type":"link","id":"cb4e186e-3fe2-40e9-9991-d2c89c919d23","label":"Live Site","url":"https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:05.845Z"}]}

Main page shows the sim running \u2014 characters walking, talking, using objects. teleportation\\n\\n### AI Decision Engine\\n\\n* LLM-driven decisions every \\\\~30 seconds per character\\n* 4 action types: interact (use object), talk (converse with character), move (walk to tile), idle (wander)\\n* AI receives: character state, nearby objects with availability, nearby characters with canTalk status\\n* Decisions include dialogue text and description\\n\\n### Smart Objects (Slot System)\\n\\n* 10 interactive objects: tea_set, kotatsu, stove, bonsai, fridge, bed, bookshelf, desk, easel, tv\\n* Each object has capacity (most=1, kotatsu=2, tv=2)\\n* Slot lifecycle: reserve (walking toward) \u2192 arrive (using) \u2192 release (done)\\n* Objects show capacity in debug UI: N/N free, pip indicators\\n* Capacity-1 blocking: second character cannot use a full object\\n* Multi-capacity: two characters can use kotatsu/tv simultaneously\\n\\n### Object State Machines\\n\\n* Each object has defined states with auto-transitions (e.g., tea_set: idle\u2192brewing\u2192ready\u2192idle)\\n* State transitions fire on interaction and auto-advance on timers\\n* All 10 objects have verified state cycles\\n\\n### Talk Handshaking\\n\\n* Character requests talk \u2192 target accepts (if idle/walking) or rejects (if interacting/talking)\\n* On accept: both enter talking state, face each other, talk timer counts down\\n* On reject: REJECT event logged with reason\\n* canTalk filter: LLM only targets available characters\\n* Talk targets distributed across all characters\\n\\n### Fallback Logic\\n\\n* When AI changes a character's plan Object reservations released on plan change\\n\\n### Debug Dashboard (/debug.html)\\n\\n* Character cards: name, position, action, target, facing, hold timer, AI countdown\\n* Object cards: name, state, slot capacity with pip indicators\\n* Event log: AI decisions, state transitions, TALK, REJECT, FALLBACK events with timestamps\\n* Speed controls: 1x, 2x, 4x\\n* Reset button: clears all state, repositions characters, resets time\\n\\n### Hold Timer\\n\\n* Characters stay at objects for the full interaction duration\\n* Timer visible in debug UI, counts down      1→// @ts-check
     2→const { defineConfig, devices } = require('@playwright/test');
     3→require('dotenv').config();
     4→
     5→const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app';
     6→
     7→module.exports = defineConfig({
     8→  testDir: './tests',
     9→  timeout: 60_000,
    10→  expect: { timeout: 15_000 },
    11→  fullyParallel: false,
    12→  retries: 1,
    13→  reporter: [['list'], ['json', { outputFile: 'test-results/results.json' }]],
    14→  use: {
    15→    baseURL: BASE_URL,
    16→    headless: true,
    17→    viewport: { width: 1280, height: 960 },
    18→    // Give Phaser/WebGL time to init
    19→    navigationTimeout: 30_000,
    20→  },
    21→  projects: [
    22→    {
    23→      name: 'chromium',
    24→      use: {
    25→        ...devices['Desktop Chrome'],
    26→        launchOptions: {
    27→          args: ['--enable-unsafe-swiftshader', '--disable-gpu'],
    28→        },
    29→      },
    30→    },
    31→  ],
    32→  outputDir: 'test-results/',
    33→});
    34→

USERMESSAGE #16
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→# AI Aquarium — M1 QA Test Plan
     2→
     3→**Milestone:** M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)
     4→**Issue:** FRG-209
     5→**Date:** 2026-03-07
     6→**Target URL:** https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html (debug dashboard) and / (Phaser page for engine inspection)
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Overview
    11→
    12→M1 validates the simulation engine's correctness, independent of Phaser rendering.
    13→The primary test target is `/debug.html` — a headless dashboard that runs the full sim
    14→and exposes character states, object states, and an event log in real time.
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Test Areas & Coverage
    19→
    20→### 1. AI Decisions Fire on Schedule (30s cycle)
    21→
    22→**Goal:** Every 30 real-time seconds, each character queries the LLM for a new action.
    23→
    24→| # | Test | Suite | File |
    25→|---|------|-------|------|
    26→| 1.1 | `decisionIntervalMs` is configured to 30000ms | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    27→| 1.2 | AI timer object initialised per character | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    28→| 1.3 | Second AI decision round fires ~30s after first | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    29→| 1.4 | Each character only receives one decision per 30s interval | regression | ai-decisions.spec.js |
    30→| 1.5 | Event log shows AI decision entries (char name + target) | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
    31→
    32→**Result (2026-03-07):** ✅ All pass. Observed cycle gap: 32s (30s interval + activity wait).
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→### 2. All Action Types Work
    37→
    38→**Goal:** `move`, `interact`, `idle` fire correctly. `talk` is a V2 action (see §2.5 below).
    39→
    40→| # | Test | Suite | File |
    41→|---|------|-------|------|
    42→| 2.1 | `idle` action observed at startup | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    43→| 2.2 | `walking` action observed within 15s | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js + character-movement.spec.js |
    44→| 2.3 | `interacting` action observed within 30s | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    45→| 2.4 | LLM `action` field only contains valid types | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    46→| 2.5 | `move` LLM decision → character walks A* path | regression | character-movement.spec.js |
    47→| 2.6 | `interact` LLM decision → object state changes | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |
    48→
    49→**Note on `talk` action:** The LLM currently does not return `action: 'talk'` in observed runs.
    50→Speech is handled via a separate `pendingSpeech` / `speechTimer` mechanism. Character-to-character
    51→conversation via a `talk` action type is not confirmed as an M1 deliverable. This is logged as
    52→a **manual verification item** below.
    53→
    54→**Result (2026-03-07):** ✅ move/interact/idle verified. talk not observed (see manual checklist).
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→### 3. Object State Transitions (idle → active → idle)
    59→
    60→**Goal:** Objects transition to an active state when used, return to idle after their timed duration.
    61→
    62→| # | Test | Suite | File |
    63→|---|------|-------|------|
    64→| 3.1 | At least one object changes state within 60s | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |
    65→| 3.2 | Debug panel reflects live object state changes | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |
    66→| 3.3 | Debug `use` button triggers object state change | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |
    67→| 3.4 | Bonsai auto-returns to idle after 12s | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |
    68→| 3.5 | Active object shows countdown timer ("Xs remaining") | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
    69→| 3.6 | Object badge in `/debug.html` updates from idle to active | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
    70→
    71→**Result (2026-03-07):** ✅ All pass. tea_set: idle → brewing observed in multiple runs.
    72→
    73→---
    74→
    75→### 4. Characters Stay at Objects for Activity Duration
    76→
    77→**Goal:** Once a character begins an activity, they remain in `interacting` state for the
    78→full `interactTimerMax` duration before cycling.
    79→
    80→| # | Test | Suite | File |
    81→|---|------|-------|------|
    82→| 4.1 | Character stays in `interacting` for ≥ half of remaining duration | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    83→| 4.2 | `interactTimerMax` is within expected range (5s–120s) | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    84→| 4.3 | Activity timer resets after completion (cycle verified) | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |
    85→| 4.4 | Character card shows "Hold Xs" countdown on `/debug.html` | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
    86→
    87→**Observed durations (real-time):**
    88→- `tea_set`: 15–30s
    89→- `stove`: 30s
    90→- `bed`: 45–60s
    91→- `bonsai`: 12s (auto_next = 12)
    92→
    93→**Result (2026-03-07):** ✅ All pass. Characters cycle activities, timer resets confirmed.
    94→
    95→**Known behavior:** After an activity completes, characters immediately begin another activity
    96→via the wander timer rather than returning to idle. This means characters can run
    97→extended activity chains. Not confirmed as a bug — may be by design.
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→### 5. Character-to-Character Conversation (talk action)
   102→
   103→**Goal:** Characters initiate dialogue with each other via a `talk` action.
   104→
   105→| # | Test | Type | Notes |
   106→|---|------|------|-------|
   107→| 5.1 | Characters have preset dialogue lines loaded | automated | speech-bubbles.spec.js ✅ |
   108→| 5.2 | AI dialogue stored on character after LLM response | automated | speech-bubbles.spec.js ✅ |
   109→| 5.3 | Speech timer fires and sets `pendingSpeech` within 45s | automated | speech-bubbles.spec.js ✅ |
   110→| 5.4 | LLM `action: 'talk'` with `targetCharacter` field | **manual** | Not observed in automated runs |
   111→| 5.5 | Speech bubble appears over both characters during talk | **manual** | Canvas rendering (M2 visual) |
   112→
   113→**Result (2026-03-07):** ✅ Speech system works. `talk` action type not yet observed from LLM.
   114→Manual verification required to confirm if `talk` action is intentionally M1 or deferred.
   115→
   116→---
   117→
   118→### 6. Pathfinding Respects Walkable Grid
   119→
   120→**Goal:** Characters navigate using A* and never walk through walls or out of bounds.
   121→
   122→| # | Test | Suite | File |
   123→|---|------|-------|------|
   124→| 6.1 | At least one character enters `walking` within 15s | regression | character-movement.spec.js |
   125→| 6.2 | Walking character tile position changes over time | regression | character-movement.spec.js |
   126→| 6.3 | Characters stay within walkable bounds (cols 1–17, rows 2–9) | regression | character-movement.spec.js |
   127→| 6.4 | `/debug.html` shows position (x, y) in character card | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   128→
   129→**Note:** Test 6.3 verifies boundary containment. Full wall-avoidance testing
   130→(characters route around interior objects) requires manual observation (canvas visual).
   131→
   132→**Result (2026-03-07):** ✅ Bounds verified. One flaky test ("idle → walking → idle" cycle
   133→20s timeout) fails occasionally — see Known Issues §8.
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→### 7. Debug Page Shows All Required Data in Real Time
   138→
   139→**Goal:** `/debug.html` provides a complete real-time view of sim state.
   140→
   141→| # | Test | Suite | File |
   142→|---|------|-------|------|
   143→| 7.1 | Dashboard loads with correct title | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   144→| 7.2 | OBJECTS section shows all 10 objects | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   145→| 7.3 | CHARACTERS section shows 3 character cards (Maple, Sol, Nyx) | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   146→| 7.4 | Character cards show Action, Mood, Energy | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   147→| 7.5 | Mood and Energy start at 100 | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   148→| 7.6 | EVENT LOG section present | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   149→| 7.7 | Event log shows AI decisions (with character name) | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   150→| 7.8 | Event log shows auto transitions | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   151→| 7.9 | Sim clock advances in real time | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   152→| 7.10 | Pause button freezes sim clock | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   153→| 7.11 | Speed controls (1x/2x/4x) visible | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   154→| 7.12 | Character card shows "AI next Xs" countdown | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   155→| 7.13 | Character card shows "Hold Xs" when interacting | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   156→| 7.14 | Object timer shows "Xs remaining" when active | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |
   157→
   158→**Result (2026-03-07):** ✅ All 19 tests pass on first run.
   159→
   160→---
   161→
   162→## Test Suite Inventory
   163→
   164→| Suite | Spec File | Tests | Status |
   165→|-------|-----------|-------|--------|
   166→| Smoke | smoke/app-loads.spec.js | 8 | ✅ 8/8 pass |
   167→| Regression | regression/ai-decisions.spec.js | 5 | ✅ 5/5 pass |
   168→| Regression | regression/character-movement.spec.js | 4 | ⚠️ 3/4 pass (1 flaky) |
   169→| Regression | regression/object-interaction.spec.js | 5 | ✅ 5/5 pass |
   170→| Regression | regression/speech-bubbles.spec.js | 4 | ✅ 4/4 pass |
   171→| **Regression (new)** | **regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js** | **19** | **✅ 19/19 pass** |
   172→| **Regression (new)** | **regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js** | **10** | **✅ 10/10 pass** |
   173→| Critical | critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js | 5 | ✅ 5/5 pass |
   174→| **Total** | | **60** | **59/60 pass** |
   175→
   176→---
   177→
   178→## Known Issues
   179→
   180→### Issue 1: "idle → walking → idle" transition test is flaky
   181→
   182→- **File:** `regression/character-movement.spec.js:62`
   183→- **Test:** `character action transitions: idle → walking → idle`
   184→- **Failure:** `pollEngineState` times out waiting for character to return to idle after walking.
   185→- **Root cause:** Characters often go directly from `walking` into `interacting` rather than `idle`,
   186→  because the wander timer fires an activity immediately on arrival. The 20s timeout is insufficient
   187→  for this path.
   188→- **Recommendation:** Extend poll timeout to 40s or widen predicate to also accept `interacting`.
   189→- **Severity:** Low — existing regression test gap, not a product bug.
   190→
   191→### Issue 2: Characters cycle activities without returning to idle
   192→
   193→- **Observed:** After finishing an interaction, characters immediately begin a new interaction
   194→  (interactTimer resets, same or new object) rather than pausing in `idle`.
   195→- **Impact:** AI decisions cannot fire for characters that are continuously interacting.
   196→  The 30s AI timer fires, but if characters are mid-interaction the decision may be queued or ignored.
   197→- **Recommendation:** Verify intended behavior: should characters have an idle period between activities?
   198→
   199→### Issue 3: `talk` action not observed in automated runs
   200→
   201→- **Observed:** LLM exclusively returned `action: 'interact'` in all M1 test runs.
   202→  `action: 'talk'` was never observed.
   203→- **Impact:** Character-to-character conversation behavior unverified.
   204→- **Recommendation:** Confirm if `talk` is an M1 deliverable or deferred to M2.
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## Manual Verification Checklist
   209→
   210→The following behaviors require visual inspection in a real browser
   211→(open `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html`):
   212→
   213→### Pathfinding
   214→
   215→- [ ] Character routes around interior walls and obstacles (not just boundary check)
   216→- [ ] Two characters can be at the same object without blocking each other's paths
   217→- [ ] Characters don't teleport — movement is smooth tile-by-tile
   218→
   219→### Visual Activity Duration
   220→
   221→- [ ] Character physically stays adjacent to the object tile for the activity duration
   222→- [ ] Character orientation (facing) matches the object direction
   223→- [ ] After activity completes, character visually departs the object area
   224→
   225→### Talk / Conversation Action
   226→
   227→- [ ] AI returns `action: 'talk'` with a `targetCharacter` field in at least one session
   228→- [ ] When `talk` fires, both characters face each other
   229→- [ ] Dialogue text appears on character card in debug dashboard
   230→
   231→### Debug Dashboard UX
   232→
   233→- [ ] Speed control 2x makes sim clock advance at 2x real-time rate
   234→- [ ] Speed control 4x makes sim clock advance at 4x real-time rate
   235→- [ ] Reset button clears all state and restarts the sim from 8:00 AM
   236→- [ ] Event log scrolls to newest entries automatically
   237→
   238→### Object Timer Accuracy
   239→
   240→- [ ] tea_set: brews for ~15–30 real seconds then returns to idle
   241→- [ ] stove: cooking for ~30 real seconds
   242→- [ ] bonsai: tending for ~12 real seconds
   243→- [ ] bed: sleeping for ~45–60 real seconds
   244→- [ ] Timer text countdown matches actual time remaining (not desynced)
   245→
   246→---
   247→
   248→## Automated Run Commands
   249→
   250→```bash
   251→# Full test gate (smoke → regression → critical)
   252→npx playwright test
   253→
   254→# M1-specific tests only
   255→npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js
   256→
   257→# Smoke only (fastest validation)
   258→npx playwright test tests/smoke/
   259→```
   260→
   261→---
   262→
   263→## Sign-off Summary
   264→
   265→| M1 Requirement | Automated | Manual | Status |
   266→|---------------|-----------|--------|--------|
   267→| AI decisions fire on 30s schedule | ✅ | — | PASS |
   268→| move action works | ✅ | — | PASS |
   269→| interact action works | ✅ | — | PASS |
   270→| idle action works | ✅ | — | PASS |
   271→| talk action works | ❌ not observed | ⬜ pending | UNVERIFIED |
   272→| Object idle → active → idle | ✅ | — | PASS |
   273→| Activity timers correct | ✅ | ⬜ visual check | PASS (timer logic) |
   274→| Characters stay at objects | ✅ | ⬜ visual check | PASS (timer verified) |
   275→| Character-to-character talk | ❌ not observed | ⬜ pending | UNVERIFIED |
   276→| Pathfinding in bounds | ✅ | ⬜ wall check | PASS (bounds) |
   277→| Debug: character states | ✅ | — | PASS |
   278→| Debug: object states | ✅ | — | PASS |
   279→| Debug: event log (decision log) | ✅ | — | PASS |
   280→| Debug: real-time updates | ✅ | — | PASS |
   281→
   282→**Overall M1 verdict:** CONDITIONAL PASS — core sim engine is correct. `talk` action and visual
   283→pathfinding quality require manual verification before full sign-off.
   284→

AI Decisions Fire on Schedule (30s cycle)\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192**Goal:** Every 30 real-time seconds, each character queries the LLM for a new action.\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192| # | Test | Suite | File |\n    25\u2192|---|------|-------|------|\n    26\u2192| 1.1 | `decisionIntervalMs` is configured to 30000ms | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    27\u2192| 1.2 | AI timer object initialised per character | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    28\u2192| 1.3 | Second AI decision round fires ~30s after first | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    29\u2192| 1.4 | Each character only receives one decision per 30s interval | regression | ai-decisions.spec.js |\n    30\u2192| 1.5 | Event log shows AI decision entries (char name + target) | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192**Result (2026-03-07):** \u2705 All pass. Observed cycle gap: 32s (30s interval + activity wait).\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192---\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192### 2. All Action Types Work\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192**Goal:** `move`, `interact`, `idle` fire correctly. `talk` is a V2 action (see \u00a72.5 below).\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192| # | Test | Suite | File |\n    41\u2192|---|------|-------|------|\n    42\u2192| 2.1 | `idle` action observed at startup | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    43\u2192| 2.2 | `walking` action observed within 15s | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js + character-movement.spec.js |\n    44\u2192| 2.3 | `interacting` action observed within 30s | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    45\u2192| 2.4 | LLM `action` field only contains valid types | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    46\u2192| 2.5 | `move` LLM decision \u2192 character walks A* path | regression | character-movement.spec.js |\n    47\u2192| 2.6 | `interact` LLM decision \u2192 object state changes | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192**Note on `talk` action:** The LLM currently does not return `action: 'talk'` in observed runs.\n    50\u2192Speech is handled via a separate `pendingSpeech` / `speechTimer` mechanism. Character-to-character\n    51\u2192conversation via a `talk` action type is not confirmed as an M1 deliverable. This is logged as\n    52\u2192a **manual verification item** below.\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192**Result (2026-03-07):** \u2705 move/interact/idle verified. talk not observed (see manual checklist).\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192---\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192### 3. Object State Transitions (idle \u2192 active \u2192 idle)\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192**Goal:** Objects transition to an active state when used, return to idle after their timed duration.\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192| # | Test | Suite | File |\n    63\u2192|---|------|-------|------|\n    64\u2192| 3.1 | At least one object changes state within 60s | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |\n    65\u2192| 3.2 | Debug panel reflects live object state changes | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |\n    66\u2192| 3.3 | Debug `use` button triggers object state change | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |\n    67\u2192| 3.4 | Bonsai auto-returns to idle after 12s | regression | object-interaction.spec.js |\n    68\u2192| 3.5 | Active object shows countdown timer (\"Xs remaining\") | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n    69\u2192| 3.6 | Object badge in `/debug.html` updates from idle to active | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192**Result (2026-03-07):** \u2705 All pass. tea_set: idle \u2192 brewing observed in multiple runs.\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192---\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192### 4. Characters Stay at Objects for Activity Duration\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192**Goal:** Once a character begins an activity, they remain in `interacting` state for the\n    78\u2192full `interactTimerMax` duration before cycling.\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192| # | Test | Suite | File |\n    81\u2192|---|------|-------|------|\n    82\u2192| 4.1 | Character stays in `interacting` for \u2265 half of remaining duration | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    83\u2192| 4.2 | `interactTimerMax` is within expected range (5s\u2013120s) | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    84\u2192| 4.3 | Activity timer resets after completion (cycle verified) | regression | m1-activity-duration.spec.js |\n    85\u2192| 4.4 | Character card shows \"Hold Xs\" countdown on `/debug.html` | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192**Observed durations (real-time):**\n    88\u2192- `tea_set`: 15\u201330s\n    89\u2192- `stove`: 30s\n    90\u2192- `bed`: 45\u201360s\n    91\u2192- `bonsai`: 12s (auto_next = 12)\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192**Result (2026-03-07):** \u2705 All pass. Characters cycle activities, timer resets confirmed.\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192**Known behavior:** After an activity completes, characters immediately begin another activity\n    96\u2192via the wander timer rather than returning to idle. This means characters can run\n    97\u2192extended activity chains. Not confirmed as a bug \u2014 may be by design.\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192---\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192### 5. Character-to-Character Conversation (talk action)\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192**Goal:** Characters initiate dialogue with each other via a `talk` action.\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192| # | Test | Type | Notes |\n   106\u2192|---|------|------|-------|\n   107\u2192| 5.1 | Characters have preset dialogue lines loaded | automated | speech-bubbles.spec.js \u2705 |\n   108\u2192| 5.2 | AI dialogue stored on character after LLM response | automated | speech-bubbles.spec.js \u2705 |\n   109\u2192| 5.3 | Speech timer fires and sets `pendingSpeech` within 45s | automated | speech-bubbles.spec.js \u2705 |\n   110\u2192| 5.4 | LLM `action: 'talk'` with `targetCharacter` field | **manual** | Not observed in automated runs |\n   111\u2192| 5.5 | Speech bubble appears over both characters during talk | **manual** | Canvas rendering (M2 visual) |\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192**Result (2026-03-07):** \u2705 Speech system works. `talk` action type not yet observed from LLM.\n   114\u2192Manual verification required to confirm if `talk` action is intentionally M1 or deferred.\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192---\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192### 6. Pathfinding Respects Walkable Grid\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192**Goal:** Characters navigate using A* and never walk through walls or out of bounds.\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192| # | Test | Suite | File |\n   123\u2192|---|------|-------|------|\n   124\u2192| 6.1 | At least one character enters `walking` within 15s | regression | character-movement.spec.js |\n   125\u2192| 6.2 | Walking character tile position changes over time | regression | character-movement.spec.js |\n   126\u2192| 6.3 | Characters stay within walkable bounds (cols 1\u201317, rows 2\u20139) | regression | character-movement.spec.js |\n   127\u2192| 6.4 | `/debug.html` shows position (x, y) in character card | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192**Note:** Test 6.3 verifies boundary containment. Full wall-avoidance testing\n   130\u2192(characters route around interior objects) requires manual observation (canvas visual).\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192**Result (2026-03-07):** \u2705 Bounds verified. One flaky test (\"idle \u2192 walking \u2192 idle\" cycle\n   133\u219220s timeout) fails occasionally \u2014 see Known Issues \u00a78.\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192---\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192### 7. Debug Page Shows All Required Data in Real Time\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192**Goal:** `/debug.html` provides a complete real-time view of sim state.\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192| # | Test | Suite | File |\n   142\u2192|---|------|-------|------|\n   143\u2192| 7.1 | Dashboard loads with correct title | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   144\u2192| 7.2 | OBJECTS section shows all 10 objects | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   145\u2192| 7.3 | CHARACTERS section shows 3 character cards (Maple, Sol, Nyx) | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   146\u2192| 7.4 | Character cards show Action, Mood, Energy | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   147\u2192| 7.5 | Mood and Energy start at 100 | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   148\u2192| 7.6 | EVENT LOG section present | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   149\u2192| 7.7 | Event log shows AI decisions (with character name) | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   150\u2192| 7.8 | Event log shows auto transitions | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   151\u2192| 7.9 | Sim clock advances in real time | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   152\u2192| 7.10 | Pause button freezes sim clock | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   153\u2192| 7.11 | Speed controls (1x/2x/4x) visible | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   154\u2192| 7.12 | Character card shows \"AI next Xs\" countdown | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   155\u2192| 7.13 | Character card shows \"Hold Xs\" when interacting | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   156\u2192| 7.14 | Object timer shows \"Xs remaining\" when active | regression | m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js |\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192**Result (2026-03-07):** \u2705 All 19 tests pass on first run.\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192---\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192## Test Suite Inventory\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192| Suite | Spec File | Tests | Status |\n   165\u2192|-------|-----------|-------|--------|\n   166\u2192| Smoke | smoke/app-loads.spec.js | 8 | \u2705 8/8 pass |\n   167\u2192| Regression | regression/ai-decisions.spec.js | 5 | \u2705 5/5 pass |\n   168\u2192| Regression | regression/character-movement.spec.js | 4 | \u26a0\ufe0f 3/4 pass (1 flaky) |\n   169\u2192| Regression | regression/object-interaction.spec.js | 5 | \u2705 5/5 pass |\n   170\u2192| Regression | regression/speech-bubbles.spec.js | 4 | \u2705 4/4 pass |\n   171\u2192| **Regression (new)** | **regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js** | **19** | **\u2705 19/19 pass** |\n   172\u2192| **Regression (new)** | **regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js** | **10** | **\u2705 10/10 pass** |\n   173\u2192| Critical | critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js | 5 | \u2705 5/5 pass |\n   174\u2192| **Total** | | **60** | **59/60 pass** |\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192---\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192## Known Issues\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192### Issue 1: \"idle \u2192 walking \u2192 idle\" transition test is flaky\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192- **File:** `regression/character-movement.spec.js:62`\n   183\u2192- **Test:** `character action transitions: idle \u2192 walking \u2192 idle`\n   184\u2192- **Failure:** `pollEngineState` times out waiting for character to return to idle after walking.\n   185\u2192- **Root cause:** Characters often go directly from `walking` into `interacting` rather than `idle`,\n   186\u2192  because the wander timer fires an activity immediately on arrival. The 20s timeout is insufficient\n   187\u2192  for this path.\n   188\u2192- **Recommendation:** Extend poll timeout to 40s or widen predicate to also accept `interacting`.\n   189\u2192- **Severity:** Low \u2014 existing regression test gap, not a product bug.\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192### Issue 2: Characters cycle activities without returning to idle\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192- **Observed:** After finishing an interaction, characters immediately begin a new interaction\n   194\u2192  (interactTimer resets, same or new object) rather than pausing in `idle`.\n   195\u2192- **Impact:** AI decisions cannot fire for characters that are continuously interacting.\n   196\u2192  The 30s AI timer fires, but if characters are mid-interaction the decision may be queued or ignored.\n   197\u2192- **Recommendation:** Verify intended behavior: should characters have an idle period between activities?\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192### Issue 3: `talk` action not observed in automated runs\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192- **Observed:** LLM exclusively returned `action: 'interact'` in all M1 test runs.\n   202\u2192  `action: 'talk'` was never observed.\n   203\u2192- **Impact:** Character-to-character conversation behavior unverified.\n   204\u2192- **Recommendation:** Confirm if `talk` is an M1 deliverable or deferred to M2.\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192---\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192## Manual Verification Checklist\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192The following behaviors require visual inspection in a real browser\n   211\u2192(open `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html`):\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192### Pathfinding\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192- [ ] Character routes around interior walls and obstacles (not just boundary check)\n   216\u2192- [ ] Two characters can be at the same object without blocking each other's paths\n   217\u2192- [ ] Characters don't teleport \u2014 movement is smooth tile-by-tile\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192### Visual Activity Duration\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192- [ ] Character physically stays adjacent to the object tile for the activity duration\n   222\u2192- [ ] Character orientation (facing) matches the object direction\n   223\u2192- [ ] After activity completes, character visually departs the object area\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192### Talk / Conversation Action\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192- [ ] AI returns `action: 'talk'` with a `targetCharacter` field in at least one session\n   228\u2192- [ ] When `talk` fires, both characters face each other\n   229\u2192- [ ] Dialogue text appears on character card in debug dashboard\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192### Debug Dashboard UX\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192- [ ] Speed control 2x makes sim clock advance at 2x real-time rate\n   234\u2192- [ ] Speed control 4x makes sim clock advance at 4x real-time rate\n   235\u2192- [ ] Reset button clears all state and restarts the sim from 8:00 AM\n   236\u2192- [ ] Event log scrolls to newest entries automatically\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192### Object Timer Accuracy\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192- [ ] tea_set: brews for ~15\u201330 real seconds then returns to idle\n   241\u2192- [ ] stove: cooking for ~30 real seconds\n   242\u2192- [ ] bonsai: tending for ~12 real seconds\n   243\u2192- [ ] bed: sleeping for ~45\u201360 real seconds\n   244\u2192- [ ] Timer text countdown matches actual time remaining (not desynced)\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192---\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192## Automated Run Commands\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192```bash\n   251\u2192# Full test gate (smoke \u2192 regression \u2192 critical)\n   252\u2192npx playwright test\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192# M1-specific tests only\n   255\u2192npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192# Smoke only (fastest validation)\n   258\u2192npx playwright test tests/smoke/\n   259\u2192```\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192---\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192## Sign-off Summary\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192| M1 Requirement | Automated | Manual | Status |\n   266\u2192|---------------|-----------|--------|--------|\n   267\u2192| AI decisions fire on 30s schedule | \u2705 | \u2014 | PASS |\n   268\u2192| move action works | \u2705 | \u2014 | PASS |\n   269\u2192| interact action works | \u2705 | \u2014 | PASS |\n   270\u2192| idle action works | \u2705 | \u2014 | PASS |\n   271\u2192| talk action works | \u274c not observed | \u2b1c pending | UNVERIFIED |\n   272\u2192| Object idle \u2192 active \u2192 idle | \u2705 | \u2014 | PASS |\n   273\u2192| Activity timers correct | \u2705 | \u2b1c visual check | PASS (timer logic) |\n   274\u2192| Characters stay at objects | \u2705 | \u2b1c visual check | PASS (timer verified) |\n   275\u2192| Character-to-character talk | \u274c not observed | \u2b1c pending | UNVERIFIED |\n   276\u2192| Pathfinding in bounds | \u2705 | \u2b1c wall check | PASS (bounds) |\n   277\u2192| Debug: character states | \u2705 | \u2014 | PASS |\n   278\u2192| Debug: object states | \u2705 | \u2014 | PASS |\n ASSISTANTMESSAGE #21
Now I understand the issue. FRG-234 is asking me to: 1. Set up a QA regression suite for AI Aquarium M1 2. Write regression tests covering every feature in the spec 3. Tests should run against `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html` 4. Consider CI integration Looking at the project, there are already tests in place: - smoke/app-loads.spec.js - regression/ai-decisions.spec.js - regression/character-movement.spec.js - regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js - regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js - regression/object-interaction.spec.js - regression/speech-bubbles.spec.js - critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js There's also a comprehensive TEST_PLAN_M1.md already documented. Looking at the acceptance criteria for FRG-234, most of the work is actually complete—the QA repo is set up with Playwright, regression tests are written covering the major features, they run against the live URL, and I just need to finalize the CI integration piece. The main gaps I'm seeing are that I need to verify the tests pass against the current live deployment, and critically, there's no README explaining how to run tests locally or in CI. I should also check the feature coverage more carefully—while most things are tested, I'm missing some specific areas like the slot system's capacity and lifecycle behavior, the fallback logic, and parts of the talk handshaking flow. Let me start by checking what's already set up in the repo, particularly looking for GitHub Actions configuration and environment files, then run the existing tests to see where we actually stand.

    "thinking": "Now I understand the issue. FRG-234 is asking me to:\n1. Set up a QA regression suite for AI Aquarium M1\n2. Write regression tests covering every feature in the spec\n3. Tests should run against `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html`\n4. Consider CI integration\n\nLooking at the project, there are already tests in place:\n- smoke/app-loads.spec.js\n- regression/ai-decisions.spec.js\n- regression/character-movement.spec.js\n- regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js\n\n\n- regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js\n- regression/object-interaction.spec.js\n- regression/speech-bubbles.spec.js\n- critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js\n\nThere's also a comprehensive TEST_PLAN_M1.md already documented. Looking at the acceptance criteria for FRG-234, most of the work is actually complete\u2014the QA repo is set up with Playwright, regression tests are written covering the major features, they run against the live URL, and I just need to finalize the CI integration piece.\n\nThe main gaps I'm seeing are that I need to verify the tests pass against the current live deployment, and critically, there's no README explaining how to run tests locally or in CI. I should also check the feature coverage more carefully\u2014while most things are tested, I'm missing some specific areas like the slot system's capacity and lifecycle behavior, the fallback logic, and parts of the talk handshaking flow.\n\nLet me start by checking what's already set up in the repo, particularly looking for GitHub Actions configuration and environment files, then run the existing tests to see where we actually stand.",

USERMESSAGE #28
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that object state changes are visible and that interactions
     5→ * (both random-wander and AI-triggered) work correctly.
     6→ *
     7→ * Also captures the known bug: AIEngine uses `_pendingInteract` but
     8→ * SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks `targetObjectId`, so
     9→ * AI-driven interactions never fire object state transitions.
    10→ */
    11→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    12→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    13→
    14→test.describe('Regression: Object interaction and state changes', () => {
    15→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    16→    await page.goto('/');
    17→    await waitForEngine(page);
    18→  });
    19→
    20→  test('at least one object changes state within 60 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    21→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    22→    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));
    23→
    24→    const changed = await pollEngineState(
    25→      page,
    26→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]),
    27→      60_000,
    28→      1000
    29→    );
    30→
    31→    const changedObjects = changed.objectStates.filter(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]);
    32→    expect(changedObjects.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    33→    console.log('Objects that changed state:', changedObjects.map(o => `${o.id}: ${initialMap[o.id]} → ${o.state}`).join(', '));
    34→  });
    35→
    36→  test('debug panel reflects live object state changes', async ({ page }) => {
    37→    // Open debug panel
    38→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    39→
    40→    // Wait for an object to go non-idle
    41→    const changed = await pollEngineState(
    42→      page,
    43→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle'),
    44→      60_000,
    45→      1000
    46→    );
    47→
    48→    const nonIdle = changed.objectStates.find(o => o.state !== 'idle');
    49→    expect(nonIdle).toBeTruthy();
    50→
    51→    // The debug panel should reflect the new state
    52→    const stateText = page.getByText(nonIdle.state, { exact: false });
    53→    await expect(stateText).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });
    54→  });
    55→
    56→  test('debug panel "use" button triggers object state change', async ({ page }) => {
    57→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    58→
    59→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    const idleObject = initial.objectStates.find(o => o.state === 'idle');
    61→    expect(idleObject, 'Need at least one idle object').toBeTruthy();
    62→
    63→    // Find and click the "use" button for that object
    64→    // The debug panel rows are: ObjectName | stateLabel | useButton
    65→    const objectLabel = idleObject.label;
    66→    const row = page.locator(`text=${objectLabel}`).locator('..').first();
    67→    const useBtn = row.getByRole('button', { name: /use/i });
    68→
    69→    // Fall back: just find any use button
    70→    const anyUseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' }).first();
    71→    await anyUseBtn.click();
    72→
    73→    // State should change
    74→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
    75→    const after = await getEngineState(page);
    76→    const anyNonIdle = after.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle');
    77→    expect(anyNonIdle, 'No object changed state after clicking "use"').toBe(true);
    78→  });
    79→
    80→  test('object auto-transitions back to idle after timed duration', async ({ page }) => {
    81→    // Target the bonsai specifically: auto_next = 12 real seconds (fastest cycle)
    82→    // Test timeout extended to allow the full cycle
    83→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    84→
    85→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    86→
    87→    // Directly trigger bonsai via engine (avoids DOM layout uncertainty)
    88→    await page.evaluate(() => {
    89→      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');
    90→    });
    91→
    92→    // Confirm bonsai is now in 'tending'
    93→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
    94→    const activated = await getEngineState(page);
    95→    const bonsai = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');
    96→    expect(bonsai?.state, 'Bonsai should be in tending state').toBe('tending');
    97→
    98→    console.log('Bonsai transitioned to tending, waiting for auto-return to idle (12s)...');
    99→
   100→    // Wait for bonsai auto_next: idle after 12 real seconds
   101→    const returned = await pollEngineState(
   102→      page,
   103→      state => state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai')?.state === 'idle',
   104→      30_000,
   105→      500
   106→    );
   107→    const bonsaiAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');
   108→    expect(bonsaiAfter.state).toBe('idle');
   109→  });
   110→
   111→  // ── BUG DOCUMENTATION TEST ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→  test('[BUG] AI interact decision sets _pendingInteract but engine checks targetObjectId', async ({ page }) => {
   113→    // This test documents the known bug:
   114→    // AIEngine._applyDecision sets character._pendingInteract = obj.id
   115→    // SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks char.targetObjectId (never set by AI)
   116→    // Result: AI-driven "interact" decisions do NOT trigger object state changes.
   117→
   118→    const interactDecisions = [];
   119→    page.on('response', async res => {
   120→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
   121→        try {
   122→          const json = await res.json();
   123→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
   124→          if (text) {
   125→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   126→            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push(d);
   127→          }
   128→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   129→      }
   130→    });
   131→
   132→    await page.goto('/');
   133→    await waitForEngine(page);
   134→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000); // capture initial decisions
   135→
   136→    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {
   137→      // No interact decisions in this run — test is inconclusive, skip gracefully
   138→      console.log('[BUG test] No "interact" decisions captured in this run, skipping verification');
   139→      return;
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    // Verify: after an AI "interact" decision, check if _pendingInteract is set
   143→    // and if targetObjectId is NOT set (confirming the bug)
   144→    const bugCheck = await page.evaluate(() => {
   145→      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];
   146→      return chars.map(c => ({
   147→        name: c.name,
   148→        hasPendingInteract: '_pendingInteract' in c && c._pendingInteract !== undefined,
   149→        hasTargetObjectId: c.targetObjectId !== null && c.targetObjectId !== undefined,
   150→        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract,
   151→        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,
   152→      }));
   153→    });
   154→
   155→    console.log('[BUG] Character interact state:', JSON.stringify(bugCheck, null, 2));
   156→
   157→    // Document: at least one character with _pendingInteract should lack targetObjectId
   158→    // This confirms the mismatch
   159→    const bugged = bugCheck.filter(c => c.hasPendingInteract && !c.hasTargetObjectId);
   160→    if (bugged.length > 0) {
   161→      console.warn(`[BUG CONFIRMED] ${bugged.length} character(s) have _pendingInteract set but targetObjectId is null — AI interact decisions will not trigger object state changes`);
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    // The test itself passes (it's documenting, not blocking) — the regression
   165→    // suite will catch when this is fixed by expecting AI-triggered state changes.
   166→    expect(interactDecisions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1); // we got interact decisions
   167→  });
   168→});
   169→

\"use\"').toBe(true);\n    78\u2192  });\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  test('object auto-transitions back to idle after timed duration', async ({ page }) => {\n    81\u2192    // Target the bonsai specifically: auto_next = 12 real seconds (fastest cycle)\n    82\u2192    // Test timeout extended to allow the full cycle\n    83\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    // Directly trigger bonsai via engine (avoids DOM layout uncertainty)\n    88\u2192    await page.evaluate(() => {\n    89\u2192      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');\n    90\u2192    });\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    // Confirm bonsai is now in 'tending'\n    93\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    94\u2192    const activated = await getEngineState(page);\n    95\u2192    const bonsai = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n    96\u2192    expect(bonsai?.state, 'Bonsai should be in tending state').toBe('tending');\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    console.log('Bonsai transitioned to tending, waiting for auto-return to idle (12s)...');\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    // Wait for bonsai auto_next: idle after 12 real seconds\n   101\u2192    const returned = await pollEngineState(\n   102\u2192      page,\n   103\u2192      state => state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai')?.state === 'idle',\n   104\u2192      30_000,\n   105\u2192      500\n   106\u2192    );\n   107\u2192    const bonsaiAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n   108\u2192    expect(bonsaiAfter.state).toBe('idle');\n   109\u2192  });\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 BUG DOCUMENTATION TEST \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   112\u2192  test('[BUG] AI interact decision sets _pendingInteract but engine checks targetObjectId', async ({ page }) => {\n   113\u2192    // This test documents the known bug:\n   114\u2192    // AIEngine._applyDecision sets character._pendingInteract = obj.id\n   115\u2192    // SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks char.targetObjectId (never set by AI)\n   116\u2192    // Result: AI-driven \"interact\" decisions do NOT trigger object state changes.\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    const interactDecisions = [];\n   119\u2192    page.on('response', async res => {\n   120\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n   121\u2192        try {\n   122\u2192          const json = await res.json();\n   123\u2192          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n   124\u2192          if (text) {\n   125\u2192            const d = JSON.parse(text);\n   126\u2192            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push(d);\n   127\u2192          }\n   128\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   129\u2192      }\n   130\u2192    });\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   133\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   134\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10000); // capture initial decisions\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {\n   137\u2192      // No interact decisions in this run \u2014 test is inconclusive, skip gracefully\n   138\u2192      console.log('[BUG test] No \"interact\" decisions captured in this run, skipping verification');\n   139\u2192      return;\n   140\u2192    }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    // Verify: after an AI \"interact\" decision, check if _pendingInteract is set\n   143\u2192    // and if targetObjectId is NOT set (confirming the bug)\n   144\u2192    const bugCheck = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   145\u2192      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];\n   146\u2192      return chars.map(c => ({\n   147\u2192        name: c.name,\n   148\u2192        hasPendingInteract: '_pendingInteract' in c && c._pendingInteract !== undefined,\n   149\u2192        hasTargetObjectId: c.targetObjectId !== null && c.targetObjectId !== undefined,\n   150\u2192        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract,\n   151\u2192        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n   152\u2192      }));\n   153\u2192    });\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192    console.log('[BUG] Character interact state:', JSON.stringify(bugCheck, null, 2));\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192    // Document: at least one character with _pendingInteract should lack targetObjectId\n   158\u2192    // This confirms the mismatch\n   159\u2192    const bugged = bugCheck.filter(c => c.hasPendingInteract && !c.hasTargetObjectId);\n   160\u2192    if (bugged.length > 0) {\n   161\u2192      console.warn(`[BUG CONFIRMED] ${bugged.length} character(s) have _pendingInteract set but targetObjectId is null \u2014 AI interact decisions will not trigger object state changes`);\n   162\u2192    }\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    // The test itself passes (it's documenting, not blocking) \u2014 the regression\n   165\u2192    // suite will catch when this is fixed by expecting AI-triggered state changes.\n   166\u2192    expect(interactDecisions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1); // we got interact decisions\n   167\u2192  });\n   168\u2192});\n   169\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. USERMESSAGE #30
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that speech bubbles appear (characters talk via pendingSpeech).
     5→ * Speech bubbles are Phaser canvas objects, so we verify via:
     6→ *  1. Console log interception (AIEngine logs descriptions)
     7→ *  2. Engine state: pendingSpeech is set and cleared
     8→ *  3. DOM: the canvas updates (indirect)
     9→ */
    10→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    11→const { waitForEngine, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    12→
    13→test.describe('Regression: Speech bubbles and character dialogue', () => {
    14→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    15→    await page.goto('/');
    16→    await waitForEngine(page);
    17→  });
    18→
    19→  test('AI engine logs character descriptions to console', async ({ page }) => {
    20→    const descriptions = [];
    21→
    22→    page.on('console', msg => {
    23→      if (msg.type() === 'log') {
    24→        const text = msg.text();
    25→        if (text.match(/^\[(Maple|Sol|Nyx)\]/)) {
    26→          descriptions.push(text);
    27→        }
    28→      }
    29→    });
    30→
    31→    await page.goto('/');
    32→    await waitForEngine(page);
    33→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
    34→
    35→    expect(descriptions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    36→    for (const desc of descriptions) {
    37→      expect(desc).toMatch(/^\[(Maple|Sol|Nyx)\]/);
    38→    }
    39→  });
    40→
    41→  test('AI dialogue is stored on character after LLM response', async ({ page }) => {
    42→    // Characters get currentDialogue set from LLM response
    43→    // Poll until at least one character has a non-null currentDialogue
    44→    const hasSpeech = await page.waitForFunction(
    45→      () => {
    46→        const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];
    47→        return chars.some(c => c.currentDialogue !== null && c.currentDialogue !== undefined);
    48→      },
    49→      { timeout: 15_000 }
    50→    ).catch(() => null);
    51→
    52→    // currentDialogue may be brief (5s timer), so also check pendingSpeech
    53→    const hasEitherSpeech = await page.evaluate(() => {
    54→      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];
    55→      return chars.map(c => ({
    56→        name: c.label,
    57→        currentDialogue: c.currentDialogue,
    58→        pendingSpeech: c.pendingSpeech,
    59→        dialogueLines: c.dialogue,
    60→      }));
    61→    });
    62→
    63→    // Each character should have preset dialogue lines loaded
    64→    for (const c of hasEitherSpeech) {
    65→      expect(c.dialogueLines.length, `${c.name} has no dialogue lines`).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    console.log('Character speech state:', JSON.stringify(hasEitherSpeech, null, 2));
    69→  });
    70→
    71→  test('characters have preset dialogue lines defined', async ({ page }) => {
    72→    const chars = await page.evaluate(() =>
    73→      window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({
    74→        label: c.label,
    75→        lines: c.dialogue,
    76→      }))
    77→    );
    78→
    79→    const mapleChar = chars.find(c => c.label === 'Maple');
    80→    const solChar = chars.find(c => c.label === 'Sol');
    81→    const nyxChar = chars.find(c => c.label === 'Nyx');
    82→
    83→    expect(mapleChar?.lines).toContain('Perfect brew today~');
    84→    expect(solChar?.lines).toContain('The bonsai looks healthy');
    85→    expect(nyxChar?.lines).toContain('I need more paint...');
    86→  });
    87→
    88→  test('speech timer fires and sets pendingSpeech within 45 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    89→    // pendingSpeech is set AND cleared within a single RAF frame, so polling can't catch it.
    90→    // Instead, we patch the engine in-page to intercept pendingSpeech assignment via a setter.
    91→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
    92→
    93→    await page.goto('/');
    94→    await waitForEngine(page);
    95→
    96→    // Patch characters to capture speech via property descriptor trap
    97→    await page.evaluate(() => {
    98→      window._qaSpeechLog = [];
    99→      const chars = window._gameEngine.characters;
   100→      for (const char of chars) {
   101→        let _speech = char.pendingSpeech;
   102→        Object.defineProperty(char, 'pendingSpeech', {
   103→          get() { return _speech; },
   104→          set(val) {
   105→            if (val) window._qaSpeechLog.push({ name: char.label, text: val, ts: Date.now() });
   106→            _speech = val;
   107→          },
   108→          configurable: true,
   109→        });
   110→      }
   111→    });
   112→
   113→    // Now wait for speech to fire (20–40s real time per character)
   114→    await page.waitForFunction(
   115→      () => window._qaSpeechLog?.length > 0,
   116→      { timeout: 50_000, polling: 500 }
   117→    );
   118→
   119→    const log = await page.evaluate(() => window._qaSpeechLog);
   120→    console.log('Speech log:', log);
   121→
   122→    expect(log.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
   123→    for (const entry of log) {
   124→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.name);
   125→      expect(typeof entry.text).toBe('string');
   126→      expect(entry.text.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   127→    }
   128→  });
   129→});
   130→

USERMESSAGE #32
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.
     4→ * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.
     9→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    10→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    11→ */
    12→async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    13→  await page.waitForFunction(
    14→    () => window._gameEngine &&
    15→          window._gameEngine.characters &&
    16→          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,
    17→    { timeout }
    18→  );
    19→}
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Read the live engine state.
    23→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    24→ */
    25→async function getEngineState(page) {
    26→  return page.evaluate(() => {
    27→    const eng = window._gameEngine;
    28→    return {
    29→      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),
    30→      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,
    31→      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({
    32→        name: c.name,
    33→        label: c.label,
    34→        tx: c.tx,
    35→        ty: c.ty,
    36→        action: c.action,
    37→        mood: c.mood,
    38→        energy: c.energy,
    39→      })),
    40→      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({
    41→        id: o.id,
    42→        label: o.label,
    43→        state: o.state,
    44→      })),
    45→    };
    46→  });
    47→}
    48→
    49→/**
    50→ * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.
    51→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    52→ * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate
    53→ * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]
    54→ * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]
    55→ */
    56→async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {
    57→  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    58→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    59→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    if (predicate(state)) return state;
    61→    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);
    62→  }
    63→  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);
    64→}
    65→
    66→module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState };
    67→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01VEH3ZLvu8U52RN9vYqNDv9",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.\n     4\u2192 * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.\n     5\u2192 */\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192/**\n     8\u2192 * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.\n     9\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    10\u2192 * @param {number} [timeout=20000]\n    11\u2192 */\n    12\u2192async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n    13\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    14\u2192    () => window._gameEngine &&\n    15\u2192          window._gameEngine.characters &&\n    16\u2192          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,\n    17\u2192    { timeout }\n    18\u2192  );\n    19\u2192}\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192/**\n    22\u2192 * Read the live engine state.\n    23\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    24\u2192 */\n    25\u2192async function getEngineState(page) {\n    26\u2192  return page.evaluate(() => {\n    27\u2192    const eng = window._gameEngine;\n    28\u2192    return {\n    29\u2192      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),\n    30\u2192      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,\n    31\u2192      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({\n    32\u2192        name: c.name,\n    33\u2192        label: c.label,\n    34\u2192        tx: c.tx,\n    35\u2192        ty: c.ty,\n    36\u2192        action: c.action,\n    37\u2192        mood: c.mood,\n    38\u2192        energy: c.energy,\n    39\u2192      })),\n    40\u2192      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({\n    41\u2192        id: o.id,\n    42\u2192        label: o.label,\n    43\u2192        state: o.state,\n    44\u2192      })),\n    45\u2192    };\n    46\u2192  });\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/**\n    50\u2192 * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.\n    51\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    52\u2192 * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate\n    53\u2192 * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]\n    54\u2192 * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]\n    55\u2192 */\n    56\u2192async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {\n    57\u2192  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n    58\u2192  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n    59\u2192    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n    60\u2192    if (predicate(state)) return state;\n    61\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState };\n    67\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #35
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * SMOKE — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies the sim page loads, Phaser boots, and all 3 characters are present.
     5→ */
     6→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     7→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState } = require('../helpers');
     8→
     9→test.describe('Smoke: App loads and sim initialises', () => {
    10→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    11→    await page.goto('/');
    12→    await waitForEngine(page);
    13→  });
    14→
    15→  test('page title is correct', async ({ page }) => {
    16→    await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/AI Aquarium/i);
    17→  });
    18→
    19→  test('Phaser canvas renders', async ({ page }) => {
    20→    const canvas = page.locator('canvas');
    21→    await expect(canvas).toBeVisible();
    22→  });
    23→
    24→  test('sim clock is visible and ticking', async ({ page }) => {
    25→    // Clock text is rendered by Phaser into the canvas — read via engine
    26→    const state1 = await getEngineState(page);
    27→    expect(state1.simTime).toMatch(/^\d+:\d{2} (AM|PM)$/);
    28→
    29→    // Verify via raw simTimeMs (more precise than string comparison)
    30→    // simSpeed = 60 → 1 real second = 60 sim seconds
    31→    const ms0 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    32→    // Wait 5 s → expect ~300 sim seconds (5 real min) of advancement
    33→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
    34→    const ms1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    35→
    36→    expect(ms1, 'simTimeMs did not advance — Phaser update loop may not be running').toBeGreaterThan(ms0);
    37→  });
    38→
    39→  test('exactly 3 characters are loaded', async ({ page }) => {
    40→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    41→    expect(state.characters).toHaveLength(3);
    42→  });
    43→
    44→  test('characters have expected names', async ({ page }) => {
    45→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    46→    const labels = state.characters.map(c => c.label).sort();
    47→    expect(labels).toEqual(['Maple', 'Nyx', 'Sol']);
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  test('AI engine is enabled', async ({ page }) => {
    51→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    52→    expect(state.aiEnabled).toBe(true);
    53→  });
    54→
    55→  test('all 10 interactive objects are loaded', async ({ page }) => {
    56→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    57→    expect(state.objectStates).toHaveLength(10);
    58→    const ids = state.objectStates.map(o => o.id).sort();
    59→    expect(ids).toEqual([
    60→      'bed', 'bonsai', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel',
    61→      'fridge', 'kotatsu', 'stove', 'tea_set', 'tv',
    62→    ]);
    63→  });
    64→
    65→  test('debug panel opens and shows object states', async ({ page }) => {
    66→    const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });
    67→    await expect(debugBtn).toBeVisible();
    68→    await debugBtn.click();
    69→    await expect(page.getByText('OBJECT STATES')).toBeVisible();
    70→    await expect(page.getByText('Stove')).toBeVisible();
    71→  });
    72→});
    73→

USERMESSAGE #37
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that each character queries the LLM API and receives valid decisions.
     5→ */
     6→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     7→const { waitForEngine } = require('../helpers');
     8→
     9→test.describe('Regression: AI decision-making via /api/llm', () => {
    10→  test('each character sends a POST to /api/llm on page load', async ({ page }) => {
    11→    const llmRequests = [];
    12→
    13→    page.on('request', req => {
    14→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    15→        llmRequests.push(req);
    16→      }
    17→    });
    18→
    19→    await page.goto('/');
    20→    await waitForEngine(page);
    21→
    22→    // All 3 characters fire immediately (timer starts at 0)
    23→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000); // allow time for all 3 requests
    24→
    25→    expect(llmRequests.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    26→  });
    27→
    28→  test('LLM request body includes character name, position, and nearby objects', async ({ page }) => {
    29→    const bodies = [];
    30→
    31→    page.on('request', req => {
    32→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    33→        try {
    34→          bodies.push(JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}'));
    35→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
    36→      }
    37→    });
    38→
    39→    await page.goto('/');
    40→    await waitForEngine(page);
    41→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
    42→
    43→    expect(bodies.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    44→
    45→    for (const body of bodies) {
    46→      // System prompt includes a character name
    47→      expect(body.system).toMatch(/You are (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
    48→      // Includes mood and energy
    49→      expect(body.system).toMatch(/mood.*\/100.*energy.*\/100/);
    50→      // User message includes sim time, tile position, nearby objects
    51→      const userContent = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';
    52→      expect(userContent).toMatch(/Sim time:/);
    53→      expect(userContent).toMatch(/You are at tile/);
    54→      expect(userContent).toMatch(/Nearby objects:/);
    55→      // max_tokens set to 256
    56→      expect(body.max_tokens).toBe(256);
    57→    }
    58→  });
    59→
    60→  test('LLM responses are valid JSON with action field', async ({ page }) => {
    61→    const responses = [];
    62→
    63→    page.on('response', async res => {
    64→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {
    65→        try {
    66→          const json = await res.json();
    67→          // Extract content from API response (Anthropic/OpenAI shape)
    68→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text
    69→                    ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
    70→          if (text) responses.push(JSON.parse(text));
    71→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
    72→      }
    73→    });
    74→
    75→    await page.goto('/');
    76→    await waitForEngine(page);
    77→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
    78→
    79→    expect(responses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    80→
    81→    for (const decision of responses) {
    82→      expect(decision).toHaveProperty('action');
    83→      expect(['move', 'interact', 'idle']).toContain(decision.action);
    84→
    85→      if (decision.action === 'move') {
    86→        expect(decision).toHaveProperty('targetTile');
    87→        expect(typeof decision.targetTile.tx).toBe('number');
    88→        expect(typeof decision.targetTile.ty).toBe('number');
    89→      }
    90→      if (decision.action === 'interact') {
    91→        expect(decision).toHaveProperty('targetId');
    92→        expect(typeof decision.targetId).toBe('string');
    93→      }
    94→    }
    95→  });
    96→
    97→  test('each character only receives one decision per 30s interval', async ({ page }) => {
    98→    const byChar = new Map();
    99→
   100→    page.on('request', req => {
   101→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
   102→        try {
   103→          const body = JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}');
   104→          const match = (body.system || '').match(/You are (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
   105→          if (match) {
   106→            const name = match[1];
   107→            byChar.set(name, (byChar.get(name) || 0) + 1);
   108→          }
   109→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   110→      }
   111→    });
   112→
   113→    await page.goto('/');
   114→    await waitForEngine(page);
   115→
   116→    // Wait well under one interval (< 30 s) — each char should have exactly 1 request
   117→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   118→
   119→    expect(byChar.size).toBe(3); // All 3 characters sent a request
   120→    for (const [name, count] of byChar) {
   121→      expect(count, `${name} sent ${count} requests (expected 1 in first 10 s)`).toBe(1);
   122→    }
   123→  });
   124→
   125→  test('/api/llm endpoint returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON', async ({ page }) => {
   126→    const statuses = [];
   127→
   128→    page.on('response', res => {
   129→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
   130→        statuses.push(res.status());
   131→      }
   132→    });
   133→
   134→    await page.goto('/');
   135→    await waitForEngine(page);
   136→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
   137→
   138→    expect(statuses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
   139→    for (const status of statuses) {
   140→      expect(status).toBe(200);
   141→    }
   142→  });
   143→});
   144→
