expect(typeof decision.targetTile.tx).toBe('number');\n    88\u2192        expect(typeof decision.targetTile.ty).toBe('number');\n    89\u2192      }\n    90\u2192      if (decision.action === 'interact') {\n    91\u2192        expect(decision).toHaveProperty('targetId');\n    92\u2192        expect(typeof decision.targetId).toBe('string');\n    93\u2192      }\n    94\u2192    }\n    95\u2192  });\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  test('each character only receives one decision per 30s interval', async ({ page }) => {\n    98\u2192    const byChar = new Map();\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    page.on('request', req => {\n   101\u2192      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n   102\u2192        try {\n   103\u2192          const body = JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}');\n   104\u2192          const match = (body.system || '').match(/You are (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);\n   105\u2192          if (match) {\n   106\u2192            const name = match[1];\n   107\u2192            byChar.set(name, (byChar.get(name) || 0) + 1);\n   108\u2192          }\n   109\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   110\u2192      }\n   111\u2192    });\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   114\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    // Wait well under one interval (< 30 s) \u2014 each char should have exactly 1 request\n   117\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192    expect(byChar.size).toBe(3); // All 3 characters sent a request\n   120\u2192    for (const [name, count] of byChar) {\n   121\u2192      expect(count, `${name} sent ${count} requests (expected 1 in first 10 s)`).toBe(1);\n   122\u2192    }\n   123\u2192  });\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  test('/api/llm endpoint returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON', async ({ page }) => {\n   126\u2192    const statuses = [];\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    page.on('response', res => {\n   129\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n   130\u2192        statuses.push(res.status());\n   131\u2192      }\n   132\u2192    });\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   135\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   136\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    expect(statuses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n   139\u2192    for (const status of statuses) {\n   140\u2192      expect(status).toBe(200);\n   141\u2192    }\n   142\u2192  });\n   143\u2192});\n   144\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code.   }

Read TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01BXGk3TAKNKTJSHJ42urGEz",
    "name": "Read",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js"
    },

      "type": "direct"

USERMESSAGE #39
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * CRITICAL — FRG-184
     4→ * End-to-end sim loop integrity:
     5→ *   1. LLM decisions fire and are applied
     6→ *   2. Characters move (A*)
     7→ *   3. Object states change (visible in debug panel)
     8→ *   4. Sim clock advances in real-time
     9→ *   5. No console errors from simulation code
    10→ *
    11→ * Also documents the critical AI interact bug (FRG-184).
    12→ */
    13→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    14→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    15→
    16→test.describe('Critical: Full sim loop integrity (FRG-184)', () => {
    17→  test('complete sim cycle: LLM fires → character moves → object interacted', async ({ page }) => {
    18→    const llmFired = [];
    19→    const simErrors = [];
    20→
    21→    page.on('request', req => {
    22→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    23→        llmFired.push(req.url());
    24→      }
    25→    });
    26→
    27→    page.on('console', msg => {
    28→      if (msg.type() === 'error' && !msg.text().includes('favicon')) {
    29→        simErrors.push(msg.text());
    30→      }
    31→      // Catch AI engine warnings about bad responses
    32→      if (msg.type() === 'warn' && msg.text().includes('AIEngine')) {
    33→        simErrors.push(`[AIEngine WARN] ${msg.text()}`);
    34→      }
    35→    });
    36→
    37→    await page.goto('/');
    38→    await waitForEngine(page);
    39→
    40→    // Step 1: LLM decisions fire
    41→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
    42→    expect(llmFired.length, 'Expected at least 3 LLM requests').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    43→
    44→    // Step 2: Characters move
    45→    const walkState = await pollEngineState(
    46→      page,
    47→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    48→      15_000
    49→    );
    50→    expect(walkState.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);
    51→
    52→    // Step 3: An object changes state (from random wander which is always working)
    53→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    54→    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));
    55→
    56→    const changedState = await pollEngineState(
    57→      page,
    58→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]),
    59→      60_000,
    60→      500
    61→    );
    62→    const changed = changedState.objectStates.filter(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]);
    63→    expect(changed.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    64→
    65→    // Step 4: Sim clock advances
    66→    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    67→    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    68→    const t2 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    69→    expect(t2).toBeGreaterThan(t1);
    70→
    71→    // Step 5: No sim errors
    72→    const criticalErrors = simErrors.filter(e =>
    73→      !e.includes('swiftshader') && !e.includes('WebGL') && !e.includes('ReadPixels')
    74→    );
    75→    if (criticalErrors.length > 0) {
    76→      console.warn('Simulation errors found:', criticalErrors);
    77→    }
    78→    expect(criticalErrors, 'Unexpected console errors in sim').toHaveLength(0);
    79→  });
    80→
    81→  test('[FIXED FRG-184] AI interact decisions no longer set _pendingInteract — targetObjectId pathway is used', async ({ page }) => {
    82→    /**
    83→     * Previously broken: AIEngine._applyDecision (interact case) set character._pendingInteract
    84→     * instead of character.targetObjectId, so SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival never fired.
    85→     *
    86→     * FIXED: _pendingInteract is no longer set by the AI engine. The correct property
    87→     * (targetObjectId) is now used, or the interaction is handled via a different pathway.
    88→     *
    89→     * This test verifies the OLD broken behavior is gone: after an AI "interact" decision,
    90→     * _pendingInteract must NOT be set on any character.
    91→     */
    92→    const interactDecisions = [];
    93→
    94→    page.on('response', async res => {
    95→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
    96→        try {
    97→          const json = await res.json();
    98→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
    99→          if (text) {
   100→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   101→            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push({ ...d, ts: Date.now() });
   102→          }
   103→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   104→      }
   105→    });
   106→
   107→    await page.goto('/');
   108→    await waitForEngine(page);
   109→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   110→
   111→    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {
   112→      console.log('No AI "interact" decisions in this run — skipping bug verification');
   113→      return;
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    // After AI decides to interact, verify targetObjectId is NOT set
   117→    const charState = await page.evaluate(() =>
   118→      window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({
   119→        label: c.label,
   120→        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract ?? null,
   121→        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId ?? null,
   122→      }))
   123→    );
   124→
   125→    // Verify the fix: _pendingInteract must NOT be set by the AI engine
   126→    console.log('[FIXED FRG-184] Character state after AI interact decision:');
   127→    console.log(JSON.stringify(charState, null, 2));
   128→    console.log('AI interact decisions received:', interactDecisions);
   129→
   130→    for (const char of charState) {
   131→      expect(char.pendingInteract,
   132→        `[REGRESSION] ${char.label}: _pendingInteract is set — old broken code path is back`
   133→      ).toBeNull();
   134→    }
   135→  });
   136→
   137→  test('sim clock advances (simTimeMs increases over real time)', async ({ page }) => {
   138→    // Note: In headless Playwright, RAF is throttled (~25% of real time).
   139→    // We verify the clock advances but do not enforce the exact 60x speed ratio
   140→    // (that ratio holds in a real browser; see live browser validation above).
   141→    await page.goto('/');
   142→    await waitForEngine(page);
   143→
   144→    const t0 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
   145→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000); // 5 real seconds
   146→    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
   147→
   148→    const simElapsed = t1 - t0;
   149→
   150→    // Clock must advance (not frozen)
   151→    expect(simElapsed, 'simTimeMs did not advance — game loop may be broken').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   152→
   153→    // In headless, simSpeed=60 but RAF throttles to ~25% real time.
   154→    // Minimum acceptable: >5 sim seconds of advancement per 5 real seconds (1:1 floor).
   155→    expect(simElapsed, 'sim clock advancing too slowly').toBeGreaterThan(5000);
   156→
   157→    const ratio = simElapsed / 5000;
   158→    console.log(`Sim speed ratio: ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x (expected 60x in production, ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x in headless)`);
   159→  });
   160→
   161→  test('character mood and energy are initialised at 100', async ({ page }) => {
   162→    await page.goto('/');
   163→    await waitForEngine(page);
   164→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
   165→    for (const c of state.characters) {
   166→      expect(c.mood, `${c.label} mood`).toBe(100);
   167→      expect(c.energy, `${c.label} energy`).toBe(100);
   168→    }
   169→  });
   170→
   171→  test('no 5xx errors from /api/llm', async ({ page }) => {
   172→    const errors = [];
   173→    page.on('response', res => {
   174→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.status() >= 500) {
   175→        errors.push({ url: res.url(), status: res.status() });
   176→      }
   177→    });
   178→
   179→    await page.goto('/');
   180→    await waitForEngine(page);
   181→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   182→
   183→    expect(errors, 'Server errors from /api/llm').toHaveLength(0);
   184→  });
   185→});
   186→

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #40
Now I have a clear picture of what exists. Let me assess the acceptance criteria for FRG-234: **Acceptance Criteria:** 1. Test suite covers all features listed in the project description ✅ (mostly done, some gaps) 2. Tests pass against current live deployment - Need to run 3. Clear mapping between tests and features ✅ (TEST_PLAN_M1.md exists) 4. README explains how to run tests locally and in CI - ❌ **Missing** (no README.md at all) 5. CI integration (GitHub Actions) - ❌ **Missing** (no .github directory) 6. Smart Objects (Slot System) - ❌ Not fully covered (missing capacity display, slot lifecycle transitions, capacity-blocking behavior, and multi-capacity object handling) 7. Talk Handshaking - ❌ Completely untested (no coverage for TALK/REJECT events, canTalk filtering, or talk target distribution) 8. Fallback Logic - ❌ Completely untested (no coverage for plan changes, unavailable fallbacks, alternative object selection, or reservation cleanup) 9. Debug Dashboard ✅ and Hold Timer ✅ are both covered Looking at what's still missing, I need to add tests for the slot/capacity system with multi-capacity objects, the talk handshaking flow with its event handling, and fallback behavior when plans change. I'm also noticing the test files are hitting the main page `/` but the debug dashboard tests go to `/debug.html` - I should verify which is the primary target and make sure the test coverage aligns with that. I need to update the issue status to "Test in Progress" first, then figure out what's actually testable—the slot system requires observing capacity states and blocking behavior, while talk handshaking can be verified through the debug event log. Now I'll update the issue status and run the test suite to see what's currently passing and what needs coverage.

USERMESSAGE #46
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
{"id":"1f786741-48ca-4044-87c8-cd2b15e41bab","identifier":"FRG-234","title":"Set up QA regression suite for AI Aquarium","description":"## Context

AI Aquarium M1 is complete. Before M2 starts, we need automated regression tests that verify all M1 features still work after any code change.

## What to do

1. Read the project description in Linear — it contains the cumulative feature spec (the 'Features' section). This is your test contract.
2. Set up a QA repo with the right testing framework. The debug dashboard at /debug.html is a DOM-only page (no canvas) — Playwright is recommended.
3. Write regression tests covering every feature in the spec. Each test should map to a specific feature.
4. Tests should run against the live URL: [https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html](<https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html>)
5. Consider CI integration (GitHub Actions) so tests run on every PR.

## Key Constraints

* The sim is AI-driven — character decisions are non-deterministic. Tests must account for this (wait for events, observe over time, not assert exact sequences).
* Some features require observation over multiple AI cycles (\\~30s each). Use speed controls (4x) to accelerate.
* The Chrome memory leak (FRG-226) means long-running browser sessions may OOM. Keep individual test runs concise.

## QA Repo

chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa (exists but mostly empty)

## Acceptance Criteria

* Test suite covers all features listed in the project description
* Tests pass against current live deployment
* Clear mapping between tests and features
* README explains how to run tests locally and in CI","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-234/set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-ai-aquarium","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-234-set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-ai-aquarium","createdAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:08.899Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:20.103Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":["testing"],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

        "text": "{\"id\":\"1f786741-48ca-4044-87c8-cd2b15e41bab\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-234\",\"title\":\"Set up QA regression suite for AI Aquarium\",\"description\":\"## Context\\n\\nAI Aquarium M1 is complete. Before M2 starts, we need automated regression tests that verify all M1 features still work after any code change.\\n\\n## What to do\\n\\n1. Read the project description in Linear \u2014 it contains the cumulative feature spec (the 'Features' section). This is your test contract.\\n2. Set up a QA repo with the right testing framework. The debug dashboard at /debug.html is a DOM-only page (no canvas) \u2014 Playwright is recommended.\\n3. Write regression tests covering every feature in the spec. Each test should map to a specific feature.\\n4. Tests should run against the live URL: [https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html](<https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app/debug.html>)\\n5. Consider CI integration (GitHub Actions) so tests run on every PR.\\n\\n## Key Constraints\\n\\n* The sim is AI-driven \u2014 character decisions are non-deterministic. Tests must account for this (wait for events, observe over time, not assert exact sequences).\\n* Some features require observation over multiple AI cycles (\\\\~30s each). Use speed controls (4x) to accelerate.\\n* The Chrome memory leak (FRG-226) means long-running browser sessions may OOM. Keep individual test runs concise.\\n\\n## QA Repo\\n\\nchillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa (exists but mostly empty)\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n* Test suite covers all features listed in the project description\\n* Tests pass against current live deployment\\n* Clear mapping between tests and features\\n* README explains how to run tests locally and in CI\",\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-234/set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-ai-aquarium\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-234-set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-ai-aquarium\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-07T23:22:08.899Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-07T23:22:20.103Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Todo\",\"labels\":[\"testing\"],\"attachments\":[],\"documents\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"AI Aquarium\",\"projectId\":\"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"}"
      }
    ]
  }
}
USERMESSAGE #47
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-209
     4→ * M1 Test Plan: Debug Dashboard (/debug.html)
     5→ *
     6→ * Tests the full debug dashboard which is the M1 foundation.
     7→ * The debug page runs the sim engine headlessly and provides real-time visibility
     8→ * into: character states, object states, and the event log.
     9→ *
    10→ * Target: /debug.html (not the main Phaser page — that is M2).
    11→ */
    12→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    13→
    14→/** Wait for the debug dashboard to finish loading (objects + characters grid populated). */
    15→async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    16→  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });
    17→  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });
    18→  // Wait until at least one character card renders an action value
    19→  await page.waitForFunction(
    20→    () => {
    21→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
    22→      if (cards.length < 3) return false;
    23→      // Check that character cards have content
    24→      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');
    25→    },
    26→    { timeout }
    27→  );
    28→}
    29→
    30→test.describe('M1: Debug Dashboard — /debug.html', () => {
    31→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    32→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    33→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    34→  });
    35→
    36→  // ── LAYOUT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37→
    38→  test('debug dashboard has correct page title', async ({ page }) => {
    39→    await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/AI Aquarium.*Debug/i);
    40→  });
    41→
    42→  test('OBJECTS section shows all 10 interactive objects', async ({ page }) => {
    43→    const objectIds = [
    44→      'stove', 'fridge', 'tea_set', 'kotatsu', 'tv',
    45→      'bookshelf', 'desk', 'bed', 'easel', 'bonsai',
    46→    ];
    47→    for (const id of objectIds) {
    48→      await expect(page.locator(`#obj-${id}`)).toBeVisible();
    49→    }
    50→  });
    51→
    52→  test('CHARACTERS section shows 3 character cards', async ({ page }) => {
    53→    await expect(page.locator('#char-0')).toBeVisible();
    54→    await expect(page.locator('#char-1')).toBeVisible();
    55→    await expect(page.locator('#char-2')).toBeVisible();
    56→  });
    57→
    58→  test('character cards show Maple, Sol, and Nyx', async ({ page }) => {
    59→    const names = await page.evaluate(() =>
    60→      ['char-0', 'char-1', 'char-2'].map(id => {
    61→        const card = document.getElementById(id);
    62→        return card ? card.textContent.match(/(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/)?.[1] ?? null : null;
    63→      })
    64→    );
    65→    expect(names).toContain('Maple');
    66→    expect(names).toContain('Sol');
    67→    expect(names).toContain('Nyx');
    68→  });
    69→
    70→  test('character cards display Action, Mood, and Energy fields', async ({ page }) => {
    71→    const card0Text = await page.locator('#char-0').innerText();
    72→    expect(card0Text).toMatch(/Action/i);
    73→    expect(card0Text).toMatch(/Mood/i);
    74→    expect(card0Text).toMatch(/Energy/i);
    75→  });
    76→
    77→  test('character Mood and Energy start at 100', async ({ page }) => {
    78→    for (const id of ['char-0', 'char-1', 'char-2']) {
    79→      const text = await page.locator(`#${id}`).innerText();
    80→      const moodMatch = text.match(/Mood\s+(\d+)/i);
    81→      const energyMatch = text.match(/Energy\s+(\d+)/i);
    82→      expect(moodMatch, `${id}: Mood not found`).toBeTruthy();
    83→      expect(energyMatch, `${id}: Energy not found`).toBeTruthy();
    84→      expect(parseInt(moodMatch[1]), `${id}: Mood should be 100`).toBe(100);
    85→      expect(parseInt(energyMatch[1]), `${id}: Energy should be 100`).toBe(100);
    86→    }
    87→  });
    88→
    89→  test('EVENT LOG section is present', async ({ page }) => {
    90→    await expect(page.locator('#log-section')).toBeVisible();
    91→    await expect(page.locator('#event-log')).toBeVisible();
    92→  });
    93→
    94→  test('sim clock is visible and shows AM/PM time', async ({ page }) => {
    95→    const clockText = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();
    96→    expect(clockText).toMatch(/\d+:\d{2} (AM|PM)/);
    97→  });
    98→
    99→  test('Pause and Reset controls are visible', async ({ page }) => {
   100→    await expect(page.locator('#btn-pause')).toBeVisible();
   101→    await expect(page.locator('#btn-reset')).toBeVisible();
   102→  });
   103→
   104→  test('speed buttons (1x, 2x, 4x) are present', async ({ page }) => {
   105→    await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: '1x' })).toBeVisible();
   106→    await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: '2x' })).toBeVisible();
   107→    await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' })).toBeVisible();
   108→  });
   109→
   110→  // ── REAL-TIME UPDATES ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   111→
   112→  test('sim clock advances over real time', async ({ page }) => {
   113→    const time1 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();
   114→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
   115→    const time2 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();
   116→    expect(time2, 'Sim clock did not advance — sim may be frozen').not.toBe(time1);
   117→  });
   118→
   119→  test('event log populates with AI decision entries within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
   120→    // Wait for at least one AI event in the log
   121→    await page.waitForFunction(
   122→      () => {
   123→        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');
   124→        return log && log.textContent.includes('AI');
   125→      },
   126→      { timeout: 15_000 }
   127→    );
   128→    const logText = await page.locator('#event-log').innerText();
   129→    expect(logText).toMatch(/AI/);
   130→  });
   131→
   132→  test('event log entries include character name and interaction target', async ({ page }) => {
   133→    // Wait for AI events
   134→    await page.waitForFunction(
   135→      () => {
   136→        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');
   137→        return log && (log.textContent.match(/AI/g) || []).length >= 1;
   138→      },
   139→      { timeout: 15_000 }
   140→    );
   141→    const logText = await page.locator('#event-log').innerText();
   142→    // At least one entry should mention a character name
   143→    const hasCharName = /(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/.test(logText);
   144→    expect(hasCharName, 'Event log should mention a character name').toBe(true);
   145→  });
   146→
   147→  test('event log entries include object state transitions (auto events)', async ({ page }) => {
   148→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
   149→    // Wait for an auto object state transition to appear in the log
   150→    await page.waitForFunction(
   151→      () => {
   152→        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');
   153→        return log && log.textContent.includes('auto');
   154→      },
   155→      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }
   156→    );
   157→    const logText = await page.locator('#event-log').innerText();
   158→    expect(logText).toMatch(/auto/);
   159→    // Should show idle → something transition
   160→    expect(logText).toMatch(/idle|brewing|cooking|tending|reading|watching|sleeping|working|painting/i);
   161→  });
   162→
   163→  test('object badge updates from idle to active when triggered', async ({ page }) => {
   164→    // Trigger bonsai via use button
   165→    const useBtn = page.locator('#int-bonsai button');
   166→    const bonsaiBadge = page.locator('#badge-bonsai');
   167→
   168→    // Confirm starts idle
   169→    await expect(bonsaiBadge).toHaveText('idle');
   170→    await useBtn.click();
   171→
   172→    // Badge should change from idle
   173→    await expect(bonsaiBadge).not.toHaveText('idle', { timeout: 5000 });
   174→    const newState = await bonsaiBadge.innerText();
   175→    expect(newState).not.toBe('idle');
   176→    console.log(`Bonsai transitioned to: ${newState}`);
   177→  });
   178→
   179→  test('active object shows timer countdown text ("Xs remaining")', async ({ page }) => {
   180→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
   181→    // Wait for any object to show a timer
   182→    await page.waitForFunction(
   183→      () => {
   184→        const timerTexts = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="timertext-"]');
   185→        return Array.from(timerTexts).some(el => el.textContent.includes('remaining'));
   186→      },
   187→      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }
   188→    );
   189→    const timerTexts = await page.evaluate(() =>
   190→      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^="timertext-"]'))
   191→        .filter(el => el.textContent.includes('remaining'))
   192→        .map(el => ({ id: el.id, text: el.textContent }))
   193→    );
   194→    expect(timerTexts.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   195→    console.log('Active object timers:', timerTexts);
   196→  });
   197→
   198→  test('character card shows "Hold Xs" when character is interacting', async ({ page }) => {
   199→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
   200→    // Wait for a character to show Hold text (means they are actively interacting)
   201→    await page.waitForFunction(
   202→      () => {
   203→        const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
   204→        return Array.from(cards).some(c => /Hold\s+\d+s/.test(c.textContent));
   205→      },
   206→      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }
   207→    );
   208→    const holdCards = await page.evaluate(() =>
   209→      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]'))
   210→        .filter(c => /Hold\s+\d+s/.test(c.textContent))
   211→        .map(c => {
   212→          const nameMatch = c.textContent.match(/(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
   213→          const holdMatch = c.textContent.match(/Hold\s+(\d+)s/);
   214→          return {
   215→            name: nameMatch?.[1],
   216→            hold: holdMatch?.[1] ? parseInt(holdMatch[1]) : null,
   217→          };
   218→        })
   219→    );
   220→    expect(holdCards.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   221→    for (const card of holdCards) {
   222→      expect(card.hold, `${card.name}: Hold duration should be > 0`).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   223→    }
   224→    console.log('Interacting characters:', holdCards);
   225→  });
   226→
   227→  test('character card shows "AI next Xs" countdown for upcoming decision', async ({ page }) => {
   228→    const aiNextText = await page.waitForFunction(
   229→      () => {
   230→        const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
   231→        return Array.from(cards).some(c => /AI next\s+\d+s/.test(c.textContent));
   232→      },
   233→      { timeout: 15_000 }
   234→    );
   235→    const cards = await page.evaluate(() =>
   236→      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]'))
   237→        .map(c => ({
   238→          id: c.id,
   239→          aiNext: c.textContent.match(/AI next\s+(\d+)s/)?.[1] ?? null,
   240→        }))
   241→    );
   242→    const withAiNext = cards.filter(c => c.aiNext !== null);
   243→    expect(withAiNext.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   244→    for (const c of withAiNext) {
   245→      const seconds = parseInt(c.aiNext);
   246→      expect(seconds, `${c.id}: AI next should be ≤ 30s`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(30);
   247→    }
   248→    console.log('AI next countdown:', withAiNext);
   249→  });
   250→
   251→  // ── PAUSE / RESUME ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   252→
   253→  test('Pause button stops the sim clock', async ({ page }) => {
   254→    const time1 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();
   255→    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();
   256→    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);
   257→    const time2 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();
   258→    expect(time2, 'Clock should be frozen when paused').toBe(time1);
   259→    // Resume
   260→    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();
   261→    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
   262→    const time3 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();
   263→    expect(time3, 'Clock should advance after resume').not.toBe(time2);
   264→  });
   265→});
   266→

return log && (log.textContent.match(/AI/g) || []).length >= 1;\n   138\u2192      },\n   139\u2192      { timeout: 15_000 }\n   140\u2192    );\n   141\u2192    const logText = await page.locator('#event-log').innerText();\n   142\u2192    // At least one entry should mention a character name\n   143\u2192    const hasCharName = /(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/.test(logText);\n   144\u2192    expect(hasCharName, 'Event log should mention a character name').toBe(true);\n   145\u2192  });\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192  test('event log entries include object state transitions (auto events)', async ({ page }) => {\n   148\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   149\u2192    // Wait for an auto object state transition to appear in the log\n   150\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   151\u2192      () => {\n   152\u2192        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n   153\u2192        return log && log.textContent.includes('auto');\n   154\u2192      },\n   155\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   156\u2192    );\n   157\u2192    const logText = await page.locator('#event-log').innerText();\n   158\u2192    expect(logText).toMatch(/auto/);\n   159\u2192    // Should show idle \u2192 something transition\n   160\u2192    expect(logText).toMatch(/idle|brewing|cooking|tending|reading|watching|sleeping|working|painting/i);\n   161\u2192  });\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192  test('object badge updates from idle to active when triggered', async ({ page }) => {\n   164\u2192    // Trigger bonsai via use button\n   165\u2192    const useBtn = page.locator('#int-bonsai button');\n   166\u2192    const bonsaiBadge = page.locator('#badge-bonsai');\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192    // Confirm starts idle\n   169\u2192    await expect(bonsaiBadge).toHaveText('idle');\n   170\u2192    await useBtn.click();\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    // Badge should change from idle\n   173\u2192    await expect(bonsaiBadge).not.toHaveText('idle', { timeout: 5000 });\n   174\u2192    const newState = await bonsaiBadge.innerText();\n   175\u2192    expect(newState).not.toBe('idle');\n   176\u2192    console.log(`Bonsai transitioned to: ${newState}`);\n   177\u2192  });\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192  test('active object shows timer countdown text (\"Xs remaining\")', async ({ page }) => {\n   180\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   181\u2192    // Wait for any object to show a timer\n   182\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   183\u2192      () => {\n   184\u2192        const timerTexts = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"timertext-\"]');\n   185\u2192        return Array.from(timerTexts).some(el => el.textContent.includes('remaining'));\n   186\u2192      },\n   187\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   188\u2192    );\n   189\u2192    const timerTexts = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   190\u2192      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"timertext-\"]'))\n   191\u2192        .filter(el => el.textContent.includes('remaining'))\n   192\u2192        .map(el => ({ id: el.id, text: el.textContent }))\n   193\u2192    );\n   194\u2192    expect(timerTexts.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   195\u2192    console.log('Active object timers:', timerTexts);\n   196\u2192  });\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  test('character card shows \"Hold Xs\" when character is interacting', async ({ page }) => {\n   199\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   200\u2192    // Wait for a character to show Hold text (means they are actively interacting)\n   201\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   202\u2192      () => {\n   203\u2192        const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n   204\u2192        return Array.from(cards).some(c => /Hold\\s+\\d+s/.test(c.textContent));\n   205\u2192      },\n   206\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   207\u2192    );\n   208\u2192    const holdCards = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   209\u2192      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]'))\n   210\u2192        .filter(c => /Hold\\s+\\d+s/.test(c.textContent))\n   211\u2192        .map(c => {\n   212\u2192          const nameMatch = c.textContent.match(/(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);\n   213\u2192          const holdMatch = c.textContent.match(/Hold\\s+(\\d+)s/);\n   214\u2192          return {\n   215\u2192            name: nameMatch?.[1],\n   216\u2192            hold: holdMatch?.[1] ? parseInt(holdMatch[1]) : null,\n   217\u2192          };\n   218\u2192        })\n   219\u2192    );\n   220\u2192    expect(holdCards.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   221\u2192    for (const card of holdCards) {\n   222\u2192      expect(card.hold, `${card.name}: Hold duration should be > 0`).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   223\u2192    }\n   224\u2192    console.log('Interacting characters:', holdCards);\n   225\u2192  });\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192  test('character card shows \"AI next Xs\" countdown for upcoming decision', async ({ page }) => {\n   228\u2192    const aiNextText = await page.waitForFunction(\n   229\u2192      () => {\n   230\u2192        const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n   231\u2192        return Array.from(cards).some(c => /AI next\\s+\\d+s/.test(c.textContent));\n   232\u2192      },\n   233\u2192      { timeout: 15_000 }\n   234\u2192    );\n   235\u2192    const cards = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   236\u2192      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]'))\n   237\u2192        .map(c => ({\n   238\u2192          id: c.id,\n   239\u2192          aiNext: c.textContent.match(/AI next\\s+(\\d+)s/)?.[1] ?? null,\n   240\u2192        }))\n   241\u2192    );\n   242\u2192    const withAiNext = cards.filter(c => c.aiNext !== null);\n   243\u2192    expect(withAiNext.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   244\u2192    for (const c of withAiNext) {\n   245\u2192      const seconds = parseInt(c.aiNext);\n   246\u2192      expect(seconds, `${c.id}: AI next should be \u2264 30s`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(30);\n   247\u2192    }\n   248\u2192    console.log('AI next countdown:', withAiNext);\n   249\u2192  });\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 PAUSE / RESUME \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192  test('Pause button stops the sim clock', async ({ page }) => {\n   254\u2192    const time1 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n   255\u2192    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n   256\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n   257\u2192    const time2 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n   258\u2192    expect(time2, 'Clock should be frozen when paused').toBe(time1);\n   259\u2192    // Resume\n   260\u2192    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n   261\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n   262\u2192    const time3 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n   263\u2192    expect(time3, 'Clock should advance after resume').not.toBe(time2);\n   264\u2192  });\n   265\u2192});\n SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-209
     4→ * M1 Test Plan: Activity Duration & 30s AI Cycle
     5→ *
     6→ * Verifies:
     7→ *  1. Characters stay at objects for the correct activity duration
     8→ *  2. interactTimerMax matches known object durations
     9→ *  3. AI decision interval is configured to 30s
    10→ *  4. Characters cycle through walk→interact→idle→walk
    11→ *
    12→ * Tests against the main page (which exposes window._gameEngine).
    13→ */
    14→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    15→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    16→
    17→test.describe('M1: Activity Duration & 30s AI Cycle', () => {
    18→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    19→    await page.goto('/');
    20→    await waitForEngine(page);
    21→  });
    22→
    23→  // ── AI CYCLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24→
    25→  test('AI decisionIntervalMs is configured to 30 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    26→    const intervalMs = await page.evaluate(() =>
    27→      window._gameEngine.aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs
    28→    );
    29→    expect(intervalMs, 'AI decision interval must be 30000ms (30s)').toBe(30_000);
    30→  });
    31→
    32→  test('AI timer object is initialized (keyed per character)', async ({ page }) => {
    33→    // _timers is an object keyed by character name; verify it exists as an object
    34→    const timersIsObject = await page.evaluate(() => {
    35→      const timers = window._gameEngine.aiEngine._timers;
    36→      return timers !== null && typeof timers === 'object' && !Array.isArray(timers);
    37→    });
    38→    expect(timersIsObject, 'aiEngine._timers should be an object').toBe(true);
    39→  });
    40→
    41→  test('second AI decision round fires after characters finish interacting', async ({ page }) => {
    42→    // This test does its own goto so the interceptor captures ALL requests from page load.
    43→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    44→
    45→    const requestTimestamps = [];
    46→    page.on('request', req => {
    47→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    48→        requestTimestamps.push(Date.now());
    49→      }
    50→    });
    51→
    52→    // Navigate AFTER setting up the interceptor
    53→    await page.goto('/');
    54→    await waitForEngine(page);
    55→
    56→    // Wait for first batch (all 3 chars fire within ~5s of load)
    57→    await page.waitForTimeout(8_000);
    58→
    59→    const firstBatch = requestTimestamps.length;
    60→    expect(firstBatch, 'First batch: all 3 characters should have fired').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    61→
    62→    // Wait for characters to finish interacting (up to 60s) + 30s cycle interval
    63→    // Total: up to 90s real time for second batch
    64→    await page.waitForTimeout(90_000);
    65→
    66→    const totalRequests = requestTimestamps.length;
    67→    const secondBatch = totalRequests - firstBatch;
    68→    expect(secondBatch, 'Second AI decision batch should have fired for at least 1 character').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    69→
    70→    if (secondBatch >= 1) {
    71→      const lastFirst = requestTimestamps[firstBatch - 1];
    72→      const firstSecond = requestTimestamps[firstBatch];
    73→      const gapMs = firstSecond - lastFirst;
    74→      console.log(`AI cycle gap between rounds: ${(gapMs / 1000).toFixed(1)}s (interval = 30s)`);
    75→    }
    76→    console.log(`Total LLM requests in ~100s: ${totalRequests} (first: ${firstBatch}, second: ${secondBatch})`);
    77→  });
    78→
    79→  // ── ACTIVITY DURATION ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    80→
    81→  test('character stays in interacting state for expected duration', async ({ page }) => {
    82→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    83→
    84→    // Wait for a character to start interacting
    85→    const startState = await pollEngineState(
    86→      page,
    87→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
    88→      20_000
    89→    );
    90→    const interactingChar = startState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'interacting');
    91→    expect(interactingChar, 'Expected at least one character to be interacting').toBeTruthy();
    92→
    93→    // Record entry time and check interactTimerMax
    94→    const timerInfo = await page.evaluate(name => {
    95→      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);
    96→      return {
    97→        label: char.label,
    98→        interactTimer: char.interactTimer,
    99→        interactTimerMax: char.interactTimerMax,
   100→        action: char.action,
   101→      };
   102→    }, interactingChar.label);
   103→
   104→    console.log(`${timerInfo.label} interactTimerMax: ${timerInfo.interactTimerMax}ms`);
   105→    expect(timerInfo.interactTimerMax, 'interactTimerMax should be > 0').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   106→
   107→    // Verify character stays interacting for at least half the remaining duration
   108→    // (we don't wait the full duration to keep test fast)
   109→    const halfRemaining = Math.min((timerInfo.interactTimerMax - timerInfo.interactTimer) / 2, 8000);
   110→    await page.waitForTimeout(halfRemaining);
   111→
   112→    const midState = await page.evaluate(name => {
   113→      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);
   114→      return { label: char.label, action: char.action };
   115→    }, interactingChar.label);
   116→
   117→    expect(midState.action, `${midState.label} should still be interacting mid-activity`)
   118→      .toBe('interacting');
   119→  });
   120→
   121→  test('interactTimerMax values fall within expected object duration ranges', async ({ page }) => {
   122→    test.setTimeout(60_000);
   123→
   124→    // Wait for at least one character to interact
   125→    await pollEngineState(
   126→      page,
   127→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
   128→      20_000
   129→    );
   130→
   131→    const timerValues = await page.evaluate(() =>
   132→      window._gameEngine.characters
   133→        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')
   134→        .map(c => ({
   135→          label: c.label,
   136→          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,
   137→        }))
   138→    );
   139→
   140→    for (const { label, interactTimerMax } of timerValues) {
   141→      // Known activity durations range from ~12s (bonsai) to ~60s (bed)
   142→      // All durations should be reasonable: 5s–120s
   143→      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s–120s)`)
   144→        .toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5_000);
   145→      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s–120s)`)
   146→        .toBeLessThanOrEqual(120_000);
   147→      console.log(`${label}: interactTimerMax = ${interactTimerMax / 1000}s`);
   148→    }
   149→  });
   150→
   151→  test('activity timer resets after completion (character cycles through activities)', async ({ page }) => {
   152→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   153→
   154→    // Wait for a character to start interacting
   155→    await pollEngineState(
   156→      page,
   157→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
   158→      20_000
   159→    );
   160→
   161→    // Find a character who is interacting with time remaining
   162→    const initial = await page.evaluate(() =>
   163→      window._gameEngine.characters
   164→        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')
   165→        .map(c => ({
   166→          label: c.label,
   167→          interactTimer: c.interactTimer,
   168→          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,
   169→          remaining: c.interactTimerMax - c.interactTimer,
   170→        }))
   171→        .sort((a, b) => a.remaining - b.remaining)
   172→    );
   173→
   174→    if (initial.length === 0) {
   175→      console.log('No interacting characters found, skipping');
   176→      return;
   177→    }
   178→
   179→    const target = initial[0];
   180→    const waitMs = Math.min(target.remaining + 5000, 30_000);
   181→    console.log(`Tracking ${target.label}: ${target.remaining}ms remaining, waiting ${waitMs}ms...`);
   182→
   183→    await page.waitForTimeout(waitMs);
   184→
   185→    // After waiting, verify the character's timer has reset (interactTimer < original interactTimerMax)
   186→    // This confirms the activity completed and a new cycle began
   187→    const after = await page.evaluate(label => {
   188→      const c = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === label);
   189→      return {
   190→        label: c.label,
   191→        action: c.action,
   192→        interactTimer: c.interactTimer,
   193→        interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,
   194→      };
   195→    }, target.label);
   196→
   197→    console.log(`${target.label} after activity:`, JSON.stringify(after, null, 2));
   198→
   199→    // The timer must have moved — either:
   200→    // a) reset (new activity started: interactTimer < original timer value), OR
   201→    // b) character is idle/walking (left interacting state)
   202→    if (after.action === 'interacting') {
   203→      // If still interacting, the timer must have reset (new cycle)
   204→      expect(after.interactTimer, `${target.label}: activity timer should have reset`)
   205→        .toBeLessThan(target.interactTimer + 1000); // timer reset means lower than original
   206→    } else {
   207→      // Left interacting state — that's also valid
   208→      expect(['idle', 'walking']).toContain(after.action);
   209→    }
   210→  });
   211→
   212→  // ── ACTION TYPE COVERAGE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   213→
   214→  test('idle action type is observed on characters', async ({ page }) => {
   215→    // Engine starts with characters in idle (before first decision)
   216→    const initialState = await getEngineState(page);
   217→    const idleChars = initialState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'idle');
   218→    // At start at least some characters should be idle or walking
   219→    const hasIdleOrWalking = initialState.characters.some(
   220→      c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'walking'
   221→    );
   222→    expect(hasIdleOrWalking, 'Characters should start in idle or walking state').toBe(true);
   223→  });
   224→
   225→  test('walking action type is observed within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
   226→    const state = await pollEngineState(
   227→      page,
   228→      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
   229→      15_000
   230→    );
   231→    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);
   232→  });
   233→
   234→  test('interacting action type is observed within 30 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
   235→    const state = await pollEngineState(
   236→      page,
   237→      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
   238→      30_000
   239→    );
   240→    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting')).toBe(true);
   241→  });
   242→
   243→  test('LLM response action field contains only valid action types', async ({ page }) => {
   244→    const decisions = [];
   245→
   246→    page.on('response', async res => {
   247→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {
   248→        try {
   249→          const json = await res.json();
   250→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
   251→          if (text) decisions.push(JSON.parse(text));
   252→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   253→      }
   254→    });
   255→
   256→    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);
   257→
   258→    expect(decisions.length, 'No LLM decisions received').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
   259→    const validActions = ['move', 'interact', 'idle', 'talk'];
   260→    for (const d of decisions) {
   261→      expect(validActions, `Unexpected action: ${d.action}`).toContain(d.action);
   262→    }
   263→    const actionTypes = [...new Set(decisions.map(d => d.action))];
   264→    console.log('Action types seen in LLM responses:', actionTypes);
   265→  });
   266→});
   267→

test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    15\u2192const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192test.describe('M1: Activity Duration & 30s AI Cycle', () => {\n    18\u2192  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n    19\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n    20\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n    21\u2192  });\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 AI CYCLE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192  test('AI decisionIntervalMs is configured to 30 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n    26\u2192    const intervalMs = await page.evaluate(() =>\n    27\u2192      window._gameEngine.aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs\n    28\u2192    );\n    29\u2192    expect(intervalMs, 'AI decision interval must be 30000ms (30s)').toBe(30_000);\n    30\u2192  });\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192  test('AI timer object is initialized (keyed per character)', async ({ page }) => {\n    33\u2192    // _timers is an object keyed by character name; verify it exists as an object\n    34\u2192    const timersIsObject = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    35\u2192      const timers = window._gameEngine.aiEngine._timers;\n    36\u2192      return timers !== null && typeof timers === 'object' && !Array.isArray(timers);\n    37\u2192    });\n    38\u2192    expect(timersIsObject, 'aiEngine._timers should be an object').toBe(true);\n    39\u2192  });\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  test('second AI decision round fires after characters finish interacting', async ({ page }) => {\n    42\u2192    // This test does its own goto so the interceptor captures ALL requests from page load.\n    43\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    const requestTimestamps = [];\n    46\u2192    page.on('request', req => {\n    47\u2192      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n    48\u2192        requestTimestamps.push(Date.now());\n    49\u2192      }\n    50\u2192    });\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    // Navigate AFTER setting up the interceptor\n    53\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n    54\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    // Wait for first batch (all 3 chars fire within ~5s of load)\n    57\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(8_000);\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    const firstBatch = requestTimestamps.length;\n    60\u2192    expect(firstBatch, 'First batch: all 3 characters should have fired').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    // Wait for characters to finish interacting (up to 60s) + 30s cycle interval\n    63\u2192    // Total: up to 90s real time for second batch\n    64\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(90_000);\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    const totalRequests = requestTimestamps.length;\n    67\u2192    const secondBatch = totalRequests - firstBatch;\n    68\u2192    expect(secondBatch, 'Second AI decision batch should have fired for at least 1 character').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192    if (secondBatch >= 1) {\n    71\u2192      const lastFirst = requestTimestamps[firstBatch - 1];\n    72\u2192      const firstSecond = requestTimestamps[firstBatch];\n    73\u2192      const gapMs = firstSecond - lastFirst;\n    74\u2192      console.log(`AI cycle gap between rounds: ${(gapMs / 1000).toFixed(1)}s (interval = 30s)`);\n    75\u2192    }\n    76\u2192    console.log(`Total LLM requests in ~100s: ${totalRequests} (first: ${firstBatch}, second: ${secondBatch})`);\n    77\u2192  });\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 ACTIVITY DURATION \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192  test('character stays in interacting state for expected duration', async ({ page }) => {\n    82\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    // Wait for a character to start interacting\n    85\u2192    const startState = await pollEngineState(\n    86\u2192      page,\n    87\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n    88\u2192      20_000\n    89\u2192    );\n    90\u2192    const interactingChar = startState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'interacting');\n    91\u2192    expect(interactingChar, 'Expected at least one character to be interacting').toBeTruthy();\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    // Record entry time and check interactTimerMax\n    94\u2192    const timerInfo = await page.evaluate(name => {\n    95\u2192      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);\n    96\u2192      return {\n    97\u2192        label: char.label,\n    98\u2192        interactTimer: char.interactTimer,\n    99\u2192        interactTimerMax: char.interactTimerMax,\n   100\u2192        action: char.action,\n   101\u2192      };\n   102\u2192    }, interactingChar.label);\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192    console.log(`${timerInfo.label} interactTimerMax: ${timerInfo.interactTimerMax}ms`);\n   105\u2192    expect(timerInfo.interactTimerMax, 'interactTimerMax should be > 0').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192    // Verify character stays interacting for at least half the remaining duration\n   108\u2192    // (we don't wait the full duration to keep test fast)\n   109\u2192    const halfRemaining = Math.min((timerInfo.interactTimerMax - timerInfo.interactTimer) / 2, 8000);\n   110\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(halfRemaining);\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192    const midState = await page.evaluate(name => {\n   113\u2192      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);\n   114\u2192      return { label: char.label, action: char.action };\n   115\u2192    }, interactingChar.label);\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192    expect(midState.action, `${midState.label} should still be interacting mid-activity`)\n   118\u2192      .toBe('interacting');\n   119\u2192  });\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  test('interactTimerMax values fall within expected object duration ranges', async ({ page }) => {\n   122\u2192    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    // Wait for at least one character to interact\n   125\u2192    await pollEngineState(\n   126\u2192      page,\n   127\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n   128\u2192      20_000\n   129\u2192    );\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192    const timerValues = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   132\u2192      window._gameEngine.characters\n   133\u2192        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')\n   134\u2192        .map(c => ({\n   135\u2192          label: c.label,\n   136\u2192          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n   137\u2192        }))\n   138\u2192    );\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    for (const { label, interactTimerMax } of timerValues) {\n   141\u2192      // Known activity durations range from ~12s (bonsai) to ~60s (bed)\n   142\u2192      // All durations should be reasonable: 5s\u2013120s\n   143\u2192      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s\u2013120s)`)\n   144\u2192        .toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5_000);\n   145\u2192      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s\u2013120s)`)\n   146\u2192        .toBeLessThanOrEqual(120_000);\n   147\u2192      console.log(`${label}: interactTimerMax = ${interactTimerMax / 1000}s`);\n   148\u2192    }\n   149\u2192  });\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  test('activity timer resets after completion (character cycles through activities)', async ({ page }) => {\n   152\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    // Wait for a character to start interacting\n   155\u2192    await pollEngineState(\n   156\u2192      page,\n   157\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n   158\u2192      20_000\n   159\u2192    );\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192    // Find a character who is interacting with time remaining\n   162\u2192    const initial = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   163\u2192      window._gameEngine.characters\n   164\u2192        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')\n   165\u2192        .map(c => ({\n   166\u2192          label: c.label,\n   167\u2192          interactTimer: c.interactTimer,\n   168\u2192          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n   169\u2192          remaining: c.interactTimerMax - c.interactTimer,\n   170\u2192        }))\n   171\u2192        .sort((a, b) => a.remaining - b.remaining)\n   172\u2192    );\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192    if (initial.length === 0) {\n   175\u2192      console.log('No interacting characters found, skipping');\n   176\u2192      return;\n   177\u2192    }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    const target = initial[0];\n   180\u2192    const waitMs = Math.min(target.remaining + 5000, 30_000);\n   181\u2192    console.log(`Tracking ${target.label}: ${target.remaining}ms remaining, waiting ${waitMs}ms...`);\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(waitMs);\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192    // After waiting, verify the character's timer has reset (interactTimer < original interactTimerMax)\n   186\u2192    // This confirms the activity completed and a new cycle began\n   187\u2192    const after = await page.evaluate(label => {\n   188\u2192      const c = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === label);\n   189\u2192      return {\n   190\u2192        label: c.label,\n   191\u2192        action: c.action,\n   192\u2192        interactTimer: c.interactTimer,\n   193\u2192        interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n   194\u2192      };\n   195\u2192    }, target.label);\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192    console.log(`${target.label} after activity:`, JSON.stringify(after, null, 2));\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    // The timer must have moved \u2014 either:\n   200\u2192    // a) reset (new activity started: interactTimer < original timer value), OR\n   201\u2192    // b) character is idle/walking (left interacting state)\n   202\u2192    if (after.action === 'interacting') {\n   203\u2192      // If still interacting, the timer must have reset (new cycle)\n   204\u2192      expect(after.interactTimer, `${target.label}: activity timer should have reset`)\n   205\u2192        .toBeLessThan(target.interactTimer + 1000); // timer reset means lower than original\n   206\u2192    } else {\n   207\u2192      // Left interacting state \u2014 that's also valid\n   208\u2192      expect(['idle', 'walking']).toContain(after.action);\n   209\u2192    }\n   210\u2192  });\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 ACTION TYPE COVERAGE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  test('idle action type is observed on characters', async ({ page }) => {\n   215\u2192    // Engine starts with characters in idle (before first decision)\n   216\u2192    const initialState = await getEngineState(page);\n   217\u2192    const idleChars = initialState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'idle');\n   218\u2192    // At start at least some characters should be idle or walking\n   219\u2192    const hasIdleOrWalking = initialState.characters.some(\n   220\u2192      c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'walking'\n   221\u2192    );\n   222\u2192    expect(hasIdleOrWalking, 'Characters should start in idle or walking state').toBe(true);\n   223\u2192  });\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192  test('walking action type is observed within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n   226\u2192    const state = await pollEngineState(\n   227\u2192      page,\n   228\u2192      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n   229\u2192      15_000\n   230\u2192    );\n   231\u2192    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);\n   232\u2192  });\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192  test('interacting action type is observed within 30 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n   235\u2192    const state = await pollEngineState(\n   236\u2192      page,\n   237\u2192      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n   238\u2192      30_000\n   239\u2192    );\n   240\u2192    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting')).toBe(true);\n   241\u2192  });\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192  test('LLM response action field contains only valid action types', async ({ page }) => {\n   244\u2192    const decisions = [];\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192    page.on('response', async res => {\n   247\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {\n   248\u2192        try {\n   249\u2192          const json = await res.json();\n   250\u2192          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n   251\u2192          if (text) decisions.push(JSON.parse(text));\n   252\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   253\u2192      }\n   254\u2192    });\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192    expect(decisions.length, 'No LLM decisions received').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n   259\u2192    const validActions = ['move', 'interact', 'idle', 'talk'];\n   260\u2192    for (const d of decisions) {\n   261\u2192      expect(validActions, `Unexpected action: ${d.action}`).toContain(d.action);\n   262\u2192    }\n   263\u2192    const actionTypes = [...new Set(decisions.map(d => d.action))];\n   264\u2192    console.log('Action types seen in LLM responses:', actionTypes);\n   265\u2192  });\n   266\u2192});\n   267\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that characters move via A* pathfinding (action = 'walking',
     5→ * tile coordinates change over time).
     6→ */
     7→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     8→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
     9→
    10→test.describe('Regression: Character movement via A*', () => {
    11→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    12→    await page.goto('/');
    13→    await waitForEngine(page);
    14→  });
    15→
    16→  test('at least one character enters walking state within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    17→    const finalState = await pollEngineState(
    18→      page,
    19→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    20→      15_000
    21→    );
    22→    const walking = finalState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'walking');
    23→    expect(walking.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    24→  });
    25→
    26→  test('walking character tile position changes over time', async ({ page }) => {
    27→    // Wait until someone walks
    28→    const state1 = await pollEngineState(
    29→      page,
    30→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    31→      15_000
    32→    );
    33→
    34→    const walker = state1.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking');
    35→    const startTx = walker.tx;
    36→    const startTy = walker.ty;
    37→
    38→    // Wait for movement to complete
    39→    await page.waitForTimeout(6000);
    40→    const state2 = await getEngineState(page);
    41→    const walkerAfter = state2.characters.find(c => c.name === walker.name);
    42→
    43→    // Position must have changed
    44→    const moved = walkerAfter.tx !== startTx || walkerAfter.ty !== startTy;
    45→    expect(moved, `${walker.label} did not move from (${startTx}, ${startTy})`).toBe(true);
    46→  });
    47→
    48→  test('characters stay within walkable bounds (cols 1–17, rows 2–9)', async ({ page }) => {
    49→    // Sample character positions over 10 seconds
    50→    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
    51→      await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    52→      const state = await getEngineState(page);
    53→      for (const c of state.characters) {
    54→        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    55→        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);
    56→        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);
    57→        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);
    58→      }
    59→    }
    60→  });
    61→
    62→  test('character action transitions: idle → walking → idle', async ({ page }) => {
    63→    // Observe a character through a full walk cycle
    64→    const walkStartState = await pollEngineState(
    65→      page,
    66→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    67→      15_000
    68→    );
    69→    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;
    70→
    71→    // Wait for that character to return to idle
    72→    const idleState = await pollEngineState(
    73→      page,
    74→      state => state.characters.find(c => c.name === charName)?.action === 'idle',
    75→      20_000
    76→    );
    77→    const charIdle = idleState.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);
    78→    expect(charIdle.action).toBe('idle');
    79→  });
    80→});
    81→

 69\u2192    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    // Wait for that character to return to idle\n    72\u2192    const idleState = await pollEngineState(\n    73\u2192      page,\n    74\u2192      state => state.characters.find(c => c.name === charName)?.action === 'idle',\n    75\u2192      20_000\n    76\u2192    );\n    77\u2192    const charIdle = idleState.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);\n    78\u2192    expect(charIdle.action).toBe('idle');\n    79\u2192  });\n    80\u2192});\n    81\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #53
Now I have a complete picture of the existing tests. Let me now run the existing tests to see the current state, then write new tests for coverage gaps. **Coverage Gaps (vs M1 feature spec):** 1. **Slot System / Smart Objects Capacity:** - Slot lifecycle: reserve → arrive → release - Object capacity display (N/N free, pip indicators in debug UI) - Capacity-1 blocking (second char can't use full object) - Multi-capacity objects (kotatsu, tv can hold 2 simultaneously) 2. **Talk Handshaking:** - TALK events appear in event log - REJECT events appear in event log - canTalk filter (LLM only targets available characters) - Talk targets distributed across all characters 3. **Fallback Logic:** - FALLBACK plan changed event in event log - FALLBACK unavailable event in event log - Object reservations released on plan change 4. **Missing from existing suite:** - README.md - CI/CD (GitHub Actions) I'll start by installing dependencies and running the existing tests, then tackle the three main test gaps for slots, talk handshaking, and fallback logic. After that I'll add documentation and set up a GitHub Actions workflow.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #67
The tests show:1. 16 passed, 2 flaky (retry passed) 2. Two known flaky tests: - `ai-decisions.spec.js:83` - fails because LLM returns `talk` action, but the allowlist in the test doesn't include `talk`. This was actually fixed in `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` which does include `talk`. This is a bug in the test itself. - `character-movement.spec.js:62` - the known flaky test about idle → walking → idle transition The `ai-decisions.spec.js:83` failure is interesting - the LLM DID return a `talk` action but the test doesn't expect it. I see that `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` has the right approach with `talk` in the valid actions list, so I need to update `ai-decisions.spec.js` to match. Since this is a pre-existing test and FRG-234 is asking me to get the suite passing, I'll fix both the test bug and the flaky character-movement test, then run the M1-specific tests to verify everything works.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #75
All critical tests pass. Now let me summarize what I've found: **Existing Test Results:** - Smoke: 8/8 ✅ - Regression (existing): 2 flaky tests (both pass on retry): - `ai-decisions.spec.js`: "LLM responses are valid JSON with action field" - fails because `talk` is in response but not in the allowlist. Bug in test, needs fix. - `character-movement.spec.js`: "idle → walking → idle" - known flaky test (documented in TEST_PLAN_M1.md) - M1 Regression: 29/29 ✅ - Critical: 5/5 ✅ Looking at the coverage gaps, I'm seeing several areas where the feature spec isn't fully tested: the slot system with multi-capacity objects and the reserve-arrive-release flow, talk handshaking with event logging, and fallback logic all lack test coverage. I also found a bug in the AI decisions test where `talk` isn't recognized as a valid action type even though the LLM now returns it. Beyond the test gaps, I'm missing the README and CI workflow that are explicitly required. My plan is to fix that `talk` action bug first, then tackle the flaky movement test, followed by writing three new regression test suites covering slots, talk handshaking, and fallback logic. Now I need to figure out what the debug page actually exposes for these features. Looking at the slot system, the debug UI should show capacity indicators and pip displays for objects. For the talk and fallback events, I'll need to examine how the event log formats these in the dashboard, and check what DOM elements are available to query in the existing tests. I should verify the actual DOM structure by inspecting the live debug page with Playwright rather than guessing at selectors, then write tests that use text matching to be more resilient to ID variations. For the TALK handshaking and Fallback tests, I'll check for the expected event log entries and character action states, while for the slot system I'll need to confirm how capacity information is actually displayed in the HTML. Writing pragmatic tests... I need to think through what's actually testable here. The capacity display in the debug UI is straightforward to verify—I can check for the "N/N free" text or pip indicators for kotatsu and tv. I can also confirm those objects have capacity=2 in the engine. The trickier parts are the blocking behavior and multi-capacity usage, since those depend on character movement timing that's hard to control in automated tests. Let me focus on what I can reliably observe: the capacity display itself and the event log entries for TALK, REJECT, and FALLBACK events. Writing the test suite...

    "thinking": "All critical tests pass. Now let me summarize what I've found:\n\n**Existing Test Results:**\n- Smoke: 8/8 \u2705\n- Regression (existing): 2 flaky tests (both pass on retry):\n  - `ai-decisions.spec.js`: \"LLM responses are valid JSON with action field\" - fails because `talk` is in response but not in the allowlist. Bug in test, needs fix.\n  - `character-movement.spec.js`: \"idle \u2192 walking \u2192 idle\" - known flaky test (documented in TEST_PLAN_M1.md)\n- M1 Regression: 29/29 \u2705\n\n\n- Critical: 5/5 \u2705\n\nLooking at the coverage gaps, I'm seeing several areas where the feature spec isn't fully tested: the slot system with multi-capacity objects and the reserve-arrive-release flow, talk handshaking with event logging, and fallback logic all lack test coverage.\n\nI also found a bug in the AI decisions test where `talk` isn't recognized as a valid action type even though the LLM now returns it. Beyond the test gaps, I'm missing the README and CI workflow that are explicitly required.\n\nMy plan is to fix that `talk` action bug first, then tackle the flaky movement test, followed by writing three new regression test suites covering slots, talk handshaking, and fallback logic.\n\nNow I need to figure out what the debug page actually exposes for these features. Looking at the slot system, the debug UI should show capacity indicators and pip displays for objects. For the talk and fallback events, I'll need to examine how the event log formats these in the dashboard, and check what DOM elements are available to query in the existing tests. I should verify the actual DOM structure by inspecting the live debug page with Playwright rather than guessing at selectors, then write tests that use text matching to be more resilient to ID variations. For the TALK handshaking and Fallback tests, I'll check for the expected event log entries and character action states, while for the slot system I'll need to confirm how capacity information is actually displayed in the HTML. Writing pragmatic tests...\n\nI need to think through what's actually testable here. The capacity display in the debug UI is straightforward to verify\u2014I can check for the \"N/N free\" text or pip indicators for kotatsu and tv. I can also confirm those objects have capacity=2 in the engine. The trickier parts are the blocking behavior and multi-capacity usage, since those depend on character movement timing that's hard to control in automated tests. Let me focus on what I can reliably observe: the capacity display itself and the event log entries for TALK, REJECT, and FALLBACK events. Writing the test suite...",

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #82
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/character-movement.spec.js",
  "old_string": "  test('character action transitions: idle \u2192 walking \u2192 idle', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Observe a character through a full walk cycle\n    const walkStartState = await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n      15_000\n    );\n    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;\n\n    // Wait for that character to return to idle\n    const idleState = await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.find(c => c.name === charName)?.action === 'idle',\n      20_000\n    );\n    const charIdle = idleState.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);\n    expect(charIdle.action).toBe('idle');\n  });",
  "new_string": "  test('character action transitions: idle \u2192 walking \u2192 interacting or idle', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Observe a character through a full walk cycle.\n    // Characters often go walking \u2192 interacting (wander timer fires activity on arrival)\n    // so we accept either interacting or idle as the post-walk state.\n    const walkStartState = await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n      15_000\n    );\n    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;\n\n    // Wait for that character to stop walking (arrive at destination)\n    const arrivedState = await pollEngineState(\n      page,\n      state => {\n        const c = state.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);\n        return c && c.action !== 'walking';\n      },\n      40_000\n    );\n    const charArrived = arrivedState.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);\n    expect(['idle', 'interacting', 'talking']).toContain(charArrived.action);\n  });"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
